databricks_access_control_rule_set.accounts/{account_id}/budgetPolicies/{bu: access control for a specific [budget policy](budget_policy.md)
databricks_access_control_rule_set.accounts/{account_id}/groups/{group_id}/: access control for a specific group
databricks_access_control_rule_set.accounts/{account_id}/servicePrincipals/: access control for a specific service principal
databricks_access_control_rule_set.accounts/{account_id}/tagPolicies/{tag_p: access control for a specific [tag policy](tag_policy.md)
databricks_access_control_rule_set.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_access_control_rule_set.grant_rules: The access control rules to be granted by this rule set, consisting of a set of principals and roles to be granted to them
databricks_access_control_rule_set.id: ID of the access control rule set - the same as `name`
databricks_access_control_rule_set.name: Unique identifier of a rule set. The name determines the resource to which the rule set applies. **Changing the name recreates the resource!**. Currently, only default rule sets are supported. The following rule set formats are supported:
databricks_access_control_rule_set.principals: a list of principals who are granted a role. The following format is supported: * `users/{username}` (also exposed as `acl_principal_id` attribute of `databricks_user` resource). * `groups/{groupname}` (also exposed as `acl_principal_id` attribute of `databricks_group` resource). * `servicePrincipals/{applicationId}` (also exposed as `acl_principal_id` attribute of `databricks_service_principal` resource)
databricks_access_control_rule_set.role: Role to be granted. The supported roles are listed below. For more information about these roles, refer to [service principal roles](https://docs.databricks.com/security/auth-authz/access-control/service-principal-acl.html#service-principal-roles), [group roles](https://docs.databricks.com/en/administration-guide/users-groups/groups.html#manage-roles-on-an-account-group-using-the-workspace-admin-settings-page), [marketplace roles](https://docs.databricks.com/en/marketplace/get-started-provider.html#assign-the-marketplace-admin-role) or [budget policy permissions](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/admin/usage/budget-policies#manage-budget-policy-permissions), depending on the `name` defined: * `accounts/{account_id}/ruleSets/default`
databricks_access_control_rule_set.roles/tagPolicy.assigner: Assigner of a specific tag policy
databricks_access_control_rule_set.roles/tagPolicy.creator: Creator of tag policies
databricks_access_control_rule_set.roles/tagPolicy.manager: Manager of a specific tag policy

databricks_alert.bool_value: boolean value (`true` or `false`) to compare against boolean results
databricks_alert.column: Block describing the column from the query result to use for comparison in alert evaluation:
databricks_alert.condition: Trigger conditions of the alert. Block consists of the following attributes:
databricks_alert.create_time: The timestamp string indicating when the alert was created
databricks_alert.custom_body: Custom body of alert notification, if it exists. See [Alerts API reference](https://docs.databricks.com/en/sql/user/alerts/index.html) for custom templating instructions
databricks_alert.custom_subject: Custom subject of alert notification, if it exists. This includes email subject, Slack notification header, etc. See [Alerts API reference](https://docs.databricks.com/en/sql/user/alerts/index.html) for custom templating instructions
databricks_alert.double_value: double value to compare against integer and double results
databricks_alert.empty_result_state: Alert state if the result is empty (`UNKNOWN`, `OK`, `TRIGGERED`)
databricks_alert.lifecycle_state: The workspace state of the alert. Used for tracking trashed status. (Possible values are `ACTIVE` or `TRASHED`)
databricks_alert.name: Name of the column
databricks_alert.notify_on_ok: Whether to notify alert subscribers when alert returns back to normal
databricks_alert.op: Operator used for comparison in alert evaluation. (Enum: `GREATER_THAN`, `GREATER_THAN_OR_EQUAL`, `LESS_THAN`, `LESS_THAN_OR_EQUAL`, `EQUAL`, `NOT_EQUAL`, `IS_NULL`)
databricks_alert.operand: Name of the column from the query result to use for comparison in alert evaluation:
databricks_alert.parent_path: The path to a workspace folder containing the alert. The default is the user's home folder.  If changed, the alert will be recreated
databricks_alert.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_alert.query_id: ID of the query evaluated by the alert
databricks_alert.seconds_to_retrigger: Number of seconds an alert must wait after being triggered to rearm itself. After rearming, it can be triggered again. If 0 or not specified, the alert will not be triggered again
databricks_alert.state: Current state of the alert's trigger status (`UNKNOWN`, `OK`, `TRIGGERED`). This field is set to `UNKNOWN` if the alert has not yet been evaluated or ran into an error during the last evaluation
databricks_alert.string_value: string value to compare against string results
databricks_alert.threshold: Threshold value used for comparison in alert evaluation:
databricks_alert.trigger_time: The timestamp string when the alert was last triggered if the alert has been triggered before
databricks_alert.update_time: The timestamp string indicating when the alert was updated
databricks_alert.value: actual value used in comparison (one of the attributes is required):
databricks_alert.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_app.budget_policy_id: The Budget Policy ID set for this resource
databricks_app.compute_size: A string specifying compute size for the App. Possible values are `MEDIUM`, `LARGE`
databricks_app.create_time: The creation time of the app
databricks_app.creator: The email of the user that created the app
databricks_app.default_source_code_path: The default workspace file system path of the source code from which app deployment are created. This field tracks the workspace source code path of the last active deployment
databricks_app.effective_user_api_scopes: A list of effective api scopes granted to the user access token
databricks_app.id: Id of the job to grant permission on
databricks_app.key: Key of the secret to grant permission on
databricks_app.name: The name of Genie Space. * ``permission` - Permission to grant on Genie Space. Supported permissions are `CAN_MANAGE`, `CAN_EDIT`, `CAN_RUN`, `CAN_VIEW`
databricks_app.permission: Permission to grant on database. Supported permissions are: `CAN_CONNECT_AND_CREATE`. * `genie_space` attribute
databricks_app.resources: A list of resources that the app have access to
databricks_app.scope: Scope of the secret to grant permission on
databricks_app.securable_full_name: the full name of UC securable, i.e. `my-catalog.my-schema.my-volume`
databricks_app.securable_type: the type of UC securable, i.e. `VOLUME`
databricks_app.service_principal_client_id: client_id (application_id) of the app service principal
databricks_app.service_principal_id: id of the app service principal
databricks_app.service_principal_name: name of the app service principal
databricks_app.space_id: The unique ID of Genie Space
databricks_app.update_time: The update time of the app
databricks_app.updater: The email of the user that last updated the app
databricks_app.url: The URL of the app once it is deployed
databricks_app.user_api_scopes: A list of api scopes granted to the user access token

databricks_catalog.browse_only: Indicates whether the principal is limited to retrieving metadata for the associated object through the BROWSE privilege when include_browse is enabled in the request.
databricks_catalog.connection_name: The name of the connection to an external data source.
databricks_catalog.created_at: Time at which this catalog was created, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_catalog.enable_predictive_optimization: Whether predictive optimization should be enabled for this object and objects under it.
databricks_catalog.full_name: The full name of the catalog. Corresponds with the name field.
databricks_catalog.isolation_mode: Whether the current securable is accessible from all workspaces or a specific set of workspaces.
databricks_catalog.managed_encryption_settings: Control CMK encryption for managed catalog data
databricks_catalog.name: Name of catalog.
databricks_catalog.options: A map of key-value properties attached to the securable.
databricks_catalog.owner: Username of current owner of catalog.
databricks_catalog.properties: A map of key-value properties attached to the securable.
databricks_catalog.provider_name: The name of delta sharing provider.
databricks_catalog.share_name: The name of the share under the share provider.
databricks_catalog.storage_location: Storage Location URL (full path) for managed tables within catalog.
databricks_catalog.storage_root: Storage root URL for managed tables within catalog.
databricks_catalog.updated_at: Time at which this catalog was last modified, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_catalog.updated_by: Username of user who last modified catalog.

databricks_cluster.apply_policy_default_values: When set to true, fixed and default values from the policy will be used for fields that are omitted. When set to false, only fixed values from the policy will be applied.
databricks_cluster.autoscale: Parameters needed in order to automatically scale clusters up and down based on load. Note: autoscaling works best with DB runtime versions 3.0 or later.
databricks_cluster.autotermination_minutes: Automatically terminates the cluster after it is inactive for this time in minutes. If not set, this cluster will not be automatically terminated. If specified, the threshold must be between 10 and 10000 minutes. Users can also set this value to 0 to explicitly disable automatic termination.
databricks_cluster.aws_attributes: Attributes related to clusters running on Amazon Web Services. If not specified at cluster creation, a set of default values will be used.
databricks_cluster.azure_attributes: Attributes related to clusters running on Microsoft Azure. If not specified at cluster creation, a set of default values will be used.
databricks_cluster.clone_from: When specified, this clones libraries from a source cluster during the creation of a new cluster.
databricks_cluster.cluster_cores: Number of CPU cores available for this cluster. Note that this can be fractional, e.g. 7.5 cores, since certain node types are configured to share cores between Spark nodes on the same instance.
databricks_cluster.cluster_id: Canonical identifier for the cluster. This id is retained during cluster restarts and resizes, while each new cluster has a globally unique id.
databricks_cluster.cluster_log_conf: The configuration for delivering spark logs to a long-term storage destination. Three kinds of destinations (DBFS, S3 and Unity Catalog volumes) are supported. Only one destination can be specified for one cluster. If the conf is given, the logs will be delivered to the destination every `5 mins`. The destination of driver logs is `$destination/$clusterId/driver`, while the destination of executor logs is `$destination/$clusterId/executor`.
databricks_cluster.cluster_memory_mb: Total amount of cluster memory, in megabytes
databricks_cluster.cluster_name: Cluster name requested by the user. This doesn't have to be unique. If not specified at creation, the cluster name will be an empty string. For job clusters, the cluster name is automatically set based on the job and job run IDs.
databricks_cluster.cluster_source: Determines whether the cluster was created by a user through the UI, created by the Databricks Jobs Scheduler, or through an API request.
databricks_cluster.creator_user_name: Creator user name. The field won't be included in the response if the user has already been deleted.
databricks_cluster.custom_tags: Additional tags for cluster resources. Databricks will tag all cluster resources (e.g., AWS instances and EBS volumes) with these tags in addition to `default_tags`. Notes:
databricks_cluster.default_tags: Tags that are added by Databricks regardless of any `custom_tags`, including:
databricks_cluster.driver: Node on which the Spark driver resides. The driver node contains the Spark master and the Databricks application that manages the per-notebook Spark REPLs.
databricks_cluster.driver_instance_pool_id: The optional ID of the instance pool for the driver of the cluster belongs. The pool cluster uses the instance pool with id (instance_pool_id) if the driver pool is not assigned.
databricks_cluster.driver_node_type_flexibility: Flexible node type configuration for the driver node.
databricks_cluster.driver_node_type_id: The node type of the Spark driver. Note that this field is optional; if unset, the driver node type will be set as the same value as `node_type_id` defined above.
databricks_cluster.enable_elastic_disk: Autoscaling Local Storage: when enabled, this cluster will dynamically acquire additional disk space when its Spark workers are running low on disk space.
databricks_cluster.enable_local_disk_encryption: Whether to enable LUKS on cluster VMs' local disks
databricks_cluster.executors: Nodes on which the Spark executors reside.
databricks_cluster.gcp_attributes: Attributes related to clusters running on Google Cloud Platform. If not specified at cluster creation, a set of default values will be used.
databricks_cluster.init_scripts: The configuration for storing init scripts. Any number of destinations can be specified. The scripts are executed sequentially in the order provided. If `cluster_log_conf` is specified, init script logs are sent to `<destination>/<cluster-ID>/init_scripts`.
databricks_cluster.instance_pool_id: The optional ID of the instance pool to which the cluster belongs.
databricks_cluster.is_single_node: This field can only be used when `kind = CLASSIC_PREVIEW`.
databricks_cluster.jdbc_port: Port on which Spark JDBC server is listening, in the driver nod. No service will be listeningon on this port in executor nodes.
databricks_cluster.last_restarted_time: the timestamp that the cluster was started/restarted
databricks_cluster.last_state_loss_time: Time when the cluster driver last lost its state (due to a restart or driver failure).
databricks_cluster.node_type_id: This field encodes, through a single value, the resources available to each of the Spark nodes in this cluster. For example, the Spark nodes can be provisioned and optimized for memory or compute intensive workloads. A list of available node types can be retrieved by using the :method:clusters/listNodeTypes API call.
databricks_cluster.num_workers: Number of worker nodes that this cluster should have. A cluster has one Spark Driver and `num_workers` Executors for a total of `num_workers` + 1 Spark nodes.
databricks_cluster.policy_id: The ID of the cluster policy used to create the cluster if applicable.
databricks_cluster.remote_disk_throughput: If set, what the configurable throughput (in Mb/s) for the remote disk is. Currently only supported for GCP HYPERDISK_BALANCED disks.
databricks_cluster.runtime_engine: Determines the cluster's runtime engine, either standard or Photon.
databricks_cluster.single_user_name: Single user name if data_security_mode is `SINGLE_USER`
databricks_cluster.spark_conf: An object containing a set of optional, user-specified Spark configuration key-value pairs. Users can also pass in a string of extra JVM options to the driver and the executors via `spark.driver.extraJavaOptions` and `spark.executor.extraJavaOptions` respectively.
databricks_cluster.spark_context_id: A canonical SparkContext identifier. This value *does* change when the Spark driver restarts. The pair `(cluster_id, spark_context_id)` is a globally unique identifier over all Spark contexts.
databricks_cluster.spark_env_vars: An object containing a set of optional, user-specified environment variable key-value pairs. Please note that key-value pair of the form (X,Y) will be exported as is (i.e., `export X='Y'`) while launching the driver and workers.
databricks_cluster.spark_version: The Spark version of the cluster, e.g. `3.3.x-scala2.11`. A list of available Spark versions can be retrieved by using the :method:clusters/sparkVersions API call.
databricks_cluster.spec: The spec contains a snapshot of the latest user specified settings that were used to create/edit the cluster. Note: not included in the response of the ListClusters API.
databricks_cluster.ssh_public_keys: SSH public key contents that will be added to each Spark node in this cluster. The corresponding private keys can be used to login with the user name `ubuntu` on port `2200`. Up to 10 keys can be specified.
databricks_cluster.start_time: Time (in epoch milliseconds) when the cluster creation request was received (when the cluster entered a `PENDING` state).
databricks_cluster.state_message: A message associated with the most recent state transition (e.g., the reason why the cluster entered a `TERMINATED` state).
databricks_cluster.terminated_time: Time (in epoch milliseconds) when the cluster was terminated, if applicable.
databricks_cluster.termination_reason: Information about why the cluster was terminated. This field only appears when the cluster is in a `TERMINATING` or `TERMINATED` state.
databricks_cluster.total_initial_remote_disk_size: If set, what the total initial volume size (in GB) of the remote disks should be. Currently only supported for GCP HYPERDISK_BALANCED disks.
databricks_cluster.use_ml_runtime: This field can only be used when `kind = CLASSIC_PREVIEW`.
databricks_cluster.worker_node_type_flexibility: Flexible node type configuration for worker nodes.

databricks_cluster_policy.definition: Policy definition: JSON document expressed in [Databricks Policy Definition Language](https://docs.databricks.com/administration-guide/clusters/policies.html#cluster-policy-definition). Cannot be used with `policy_family_id`
databricks_cluster_policy.description: Additional human-readable description of the cluster policy
databricks_cluster_policy.id: Canonical unique identifier for the cluster policy. This is equal to `policy_id`
databricks_cluster_policy.max_clusters_per_user: Maximum number of clusters allowed per user. When omitted, there is no limit. If specified, value must be greater than zero. * `policy_family_definition_overrides`(Optional) Policy definition JSON document expressed in Databricks Policy Definition Language. The JSON document must be passed as a string and cannot be embedded in the requests. You can use this to customize the policy definition inherited from the policy family. Policy rules specified here are merged into the inherited policy definition. * `policy_family_id` (Optional) ID of the policy family. The cluster policy's policy definition inherits the policy family's policy definition. Cannot be used with `definition`. Use `policy_family_definition_overrides` instead to customize the policy definition
databricks_cluster_policy.name: Cluster policy name. This must be unique. Length must be between 1 and 100 characters
databricks_cluster_policy.policy_id: Canonical unique identifier for the cluster policy
databricks_cluster_policy.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_cluster_policy.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_dashboard.dataset_catalog: Sets the default catalog for all datasets in this dashboard. Does not impact table references that use fully qualified catalog names (ex: samples.nyctaxi.trips)
databricks_dashboard.dataset_schema: Sets the default schema for all datasets in this dashboard. Does not impact table references that use fully qualified catalog names (ex: samples.nyctaxi.trips)
databricks_dashboard.display_name: The display name of the dashboard
databricks_dashboard.embed_credentials: Whether to embed credentials in the dashboard. Default is `true`
databricks_dashboard.file_path: The path to the dashboard JSON file. Conflicts with `serialized_dashboard`
databricks_dashboard.id: The unique ID of the dashboard
databricks_dashboard.parent_path: The workspace path of the folder containing the dashboard. Includes leading slash and no trailing slash.  If folder doesn't exist, it will be created

databricks_dashboard.warehouse_id: The warehouse ID used to run the dashboard
databricks_dashboard.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_dbfs_file.content_base64: Encoded file contents. Conflicts with `source`. Use of `content_base64` is discouraged, as it's increasing memory footprint of Terraform state and should only be used in exceptional circumstances, like creating a data pipeline configuration file
databricks_dbfs_file.file_size: The file size of the file that is being tracked by this resource in bytes
databricks_dbfs_file.path: The path of the file in which you wish to save
databricks_dbfs_file.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_dbfs_file.source: The full absolute path to the file. Conflicts with `content_base64`
databricks_dbfs_file.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_entitlements.allow_cluster_create: Allow the principal to have [cluster](cluster.md) create privileges. Defaults to false. More fine grained permissions could be assigned with [databricks_permissions](permissions.md#Cluster-usage) and `cluster_id` argument. Everyone without `allow_cluster_create` argument set, but with [permission to use](permissions.md#Cluster-Policy-usage) Cluster Policy would be able to create clusters, but within boundaries of that specific policy

databricks_entitlements.databricks_sql_access: This is a field to allow the principal to have access to [Databricks SQL](https://databricks.com/product/databricks-sql)  UI, [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one) and through [databricks_sql_endpoint](sql_endpoint.md)
databricks_entitlements.group_id: Canonical unique identifier for the group
databricks_entitlements.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_entitlements.service_principal_id: Canonical unique identifier for the service principal
databricks_entitlements.user_id: Canonical unique identifier for the user
databricks_entitlements.workspace_access: This is a field to allow the principal to have access to a Databricks Workspace UI and [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one)
databricks_entitlements.workspace_consume: This is a field to allow the principal to have access only to [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one).  Couldn't be used with `workspace_access` or `databricks_sql_access`
databricks_entitlements.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_git_credential.force: specify if settings need to be enforced (i.e., to overwrite previously set credential for service principals)
databricks_git_credential.git_email: The email associated with your Git provider user account. Used for authentication with the remote repository and also sets the author & committer identity for commits
databricks_git_credential.git_provider: case insensitive name of the Git provider.  Following values are supported right now (could be a subject for a change, consult [Git Credentials API documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/dev-tools/api/latest/gitcredentials.html)): `gitHub`, `gitHubEnterprise`, `bitbucketCloud`, `bitbucketServer`, `azureDevOpsServices`, `gitLab`, `gitLabEnterpriseEdition`, `awsCodeCommit`, `azureDevOpsServicesAad`
databricks_git_credential.git_username: user name at Git provider.  For most Git providers it is only used to set the Git committer & author names for commits, however it may be required for authentication depending on your Git provider / token requirements
databricks_git_credential.is_default_for_provider: boolean flag specifying if the credential is the default for the given provider type
databricks_git_credential.name: the name of the git credential, used for identification and ease of lookup
databricks_git_credential.personal_access_token: The personal access token used to authenticate to the corresponding Git provider. If value is not provided, it's sourced from the first environment variable of [`GITHUB_TOKEN`](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/integrations/github/latest/docs#oauth--personal-access-token), [`GITLAB_TOKEN`](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/gitlabhq/gitlab/latest/docs#required), or [`AZDO_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/microsoft/azuredevops/latest/docs#argument-reference), that has a non-empty value
databricks_git_credential.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_git_credential.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_global_init_script.content_base64: The base64-encoded source code global init script. Conflicts with `source`. Use of `content_base64` is discouraged, as it's increasing memory footprint of Terraform state and should only be used in exceptional circumstances * `enabled` (bool, optional default: `false`) specifies if the script is enabled for execution, or not * `position` (integer, optional default: `null`) - the position of a global init script, where `0` represents the first global init script to run, `1` is the second global init script to run, and so on. When omitted, the script gets the last position
databricks_global_init_script.id: ID assigned to a global init script by API
databricks_global_init_script.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_global_init_script.source: Path to script's source code on local filesystem. Conflicts with `content_base64`
databricks_global_init_script.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_group.acl_principal_id: identifier for use in [databricks_access_control_rule_set](access_control_rule_set.md), e.g. `groups/Some Group`
databricks_group.allow_cluster_create: This is a field to allow the group to have [cluster](cluster.md) create privileges. More fine grained permissions could be assigned with [databricks_permissions](permissions.md#Cluster-usage) and [cluster_id](permissions.md#cluster_id) argument. Everyone without `allow_cluster_create` argument set, but with [permission to use](permissions.md#Cluster-Policy-usage) Cluster Policy would be able to create clusters, but within boundaries of that specific policy
databricks_group.allow_instance_pool_create: This is a field to allow the group to have [instance pool](instance_pool.md) create privileges. More fine grained permissions could be assigned with [databricks_permissions](permissions.md#Instance-Pool-usage) and [instance_pool_id](permissions.md#instance_pool_id) argument
databricks_group.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_group.databricks_sql_access: This is a field to allow the group to have access to [Databricks SQL](https://databricks.com/product/databricks-sql)  UI, [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one) and through [databricks_sql_endpoint](sql_endpoint.md)
databricks_group.display_name: This is the display name for the given group
databricks_group.external_id: ID of the group in an external identity provider
databricks_group.force: Ignore `cannot create group: Group with name X already exists.` errors and implicitly import the specific group into Terraform state, enforcing entitlements defined in the instance of resource. _This functionality is experimental_ and is designed to simplify corner cases, like Azure Active Directory synchronisation
databricks_group.id: Canonical unique identifier for the group (SCIM ID)
databricks_group.workspace_access: This is a field to allow the group to have access to a Databricks Workspace UI and [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one)
databricks_group.workspace_consume: This is a field to allow the group to have access only to [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one).  Couldn't be used with `workspace_access` or `databricks_sql_access`

databricks_group_member.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_group_member.group_id: This is the `id` attribute (SCIM ID) of the [group](group.md) resource
databricks_group_member.id: The id for the `databricks_group_member` object which is in the format `<group_id>|<member_id>`
databricks_group_member.member_id: This is the `id` attribute (SCIM ID) of the [group](group.md), [service principal](service_principal.md), or [user](user.md)

databricks_instance_pool.alternate_node_type_ids: list of alternative node types that will be used if main node type isn't available.  Follow the [documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/compute/flexible-node-types#fallback-instance-type-requirements) for requirements on selection of alternative node types
databricks_instance_pool.availability: Availability type used for all nodes. Valid values are `SPOT_AZURE` and `ON_DEMAND_AZURE`
databricks_instance_pool.basic_auth: `basic_auth.username` and `basic_auth.password` for Docker repository. Docker registry credentials are encrypted when they are stored in Databricks internal storage and when they are passed to a registry upon fetching Docker images at cluster launch.  For better security, these credentials should be stored in the secret scope and referred using secret path syntax: `{{secrets/scope/key}}`, otherwise other users of the workspace may access them via UI/API
databricks_instance_pool.custom_tags: (Map) Additional tags for instance pool resources. Databricks tags all pool resources (e.g. AWS & Azure instances and Disk volumes). The tags of the instance pool will propagate to the clusters using the pool (see the [official documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/administration-guide/account-settings/usage-detail-tags-aws.html#tag-propagation)). Attempting to set the same tags in both cluster and instance pool will raise an error. *Databricks allows at most 43 custom tags.*
databricks_instance_pool.disk_count: (Integer) The number of disks to attach to each instance. This feature is only enabled for supported node types. Users can choose up to the limit of the disks supported by the node type. For node types with no local disk, at least one disk needs to be specified
databricks_instance_pool.disk_size: (Integer) The size of each disk (in GiB) to attach
databricks_instance_pool.enable_elastic_disk: (Bool) Autoscaling Local Storage: when enabled, the instances in the pool dynamically acquire additional disk space when they are running low on disk space
databricks_instance_pool.gcp_availability: Availability type used for all nodes. Valid values are `PREEMPTIBLE_GCP`, `PREEMPTIBLE_WITH_FALLBACK_GCP` and `ON_DEMAND_GCP`, default: `ON_DEMAND_GCP`. * `local_ssd_count` (Optional, Int) Number of local SSD disks (each is 375GB in size) that will be attached to each node of the cluster
databricks_instance_pool.id: Canonical unique identifier for the instance pool
databricks_instance_pool.idle_instance_autotermination_minutes: (Integer) The number of minutes that idle instances in excess of the min_idle_instances are maintained by the pool before being terminated. If not specified, excess idle instances are terminated automatically after a default timeout period. If specified, the time must be between 0 and 10000 minutes. If you specify 0, excess idle instances are removed as soon as possible
databricks_instance_pool.instance_pool_name: (String) The name of the instance pool. This is required for create and edit operations. It must be unique, non-empty, and less than 100 characters
databricks_instance_pool.instance_profile_arn: Nodes belonging to the pool will only be placed on AWS instances with this instance profile. Please see [databricks_instance_profile](instance_profile.md) resource documentation for extended examples on adding a valid instance profile using Terraform
databricks_instance_pool.max_capacity: (Integer) The maximum number of instances the pool can contain, including both idle instances and ones in use by clusters. Once the maximum capacity is reached, you cannot create new clusters from the pool and existing clusters cannot autoscale up until some instances are made idle in the pool via [cluster](cluster.md) termination or down-scaling. There is no default limit, but as a [best practice](https://docs.databricks.com/clusters/instance-pools/pool-best-practices.html#configure-pools-to-control-cost), this should be set based on anticipated usage
databricks_instance_pool.min_idle_instances: (Integer) The minimum number of idle instances maintained by the pool. This is in addition to any instances in use by active clusters
databricks_instance_pool.node_type_flexibility: a block describing the alternative driver node types if `node_type_id` isn't available
databricks_instance_pool.node_type_id: (String) The node type for the instances in the pool. All clusters attached to the pool inherit this node type and the pool's idle instances are allocated based on this type. You can retrieve a list of available node types by using the [List Node Types API](https://docs.databricks.com/dev-tools/api/latest/clusters.html#clusterclusterservicelistnodetypes) call
databricks_instance_pool.preloaded_spark_versions: (List) A list with at most one runtime version the pool installs on each instance. Pool clusters that use a preloaded runtime version start faster as they do not have to wait for the image to download. You can retrieve them via [databricks_spark_version](../data-sources/spark_version.md) data source or via  [Runtime Versions API](https://docs.databricks.com/dev-tools/api/latest/clusters.html#clusterclusterservicelistsparkversions) call
databricks_instance_pool.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_instance_pool.spot_bid_max_price: The max bid price used for Azure spot instances. You can set this to greater than or equal to the current spot price. You can also set this to `-1`, which specifies that the instance cannot be evicted on the basis of price. The price for the instance will be the current price for spot instances or the price for a standard instance
databricks_instance_pool.spot_bid_price_percent: (Integer) The max price for AWS spot instances, as a percentage of the corresponding instance type's on-demand price. For example, if this field is set to 50, and the instance pool needs a new i3.xlarge spot instance, then the max price is half of the price of on-demand i3.xlarge instances. Similarly, if this field is set to 200, the max price is twice the price of on-demand i3.xlarge instances. If not specified, the *default value is 100*. When spot instances are requested for this instance pool, only spot instances whose max price percentage matches this field are considered. *For safety, this field cannot be greater than 10000.*
databricks_instance_pool.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with
databricks_instance_pool.zone_id: Identifier for the availability zone/datacenter in which the cluster resides. This string will be of a form like `us-central1-a`. The provided availability zone must be in the same region as the Databricks workspace

databricks_ip_access_list.enabled: Boolean `true` or `false` indicating whether this list should be active.  Defaults to `true`
databricks_ip_access_list.id: Canonical unique identifier for the IP Access List, same as `list_id`
databricks_ip_access_list.ip_addresses: A string list of IP addresses and CIDR ranges
databricks_ip_access_list.label: This is the display name for the given IP ACL List
databricks_ip_access_list.list_id: Canonical unique identifier for the IP Access List
databricks_ip_access_list.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_ip_access_list.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_job.*_task: one of the specific task blocks described below: * `clean_rooms_notebook_task` * `condition_task` * `dashboard_task` * `dbt_task` * `for_each_task` * `notebook_task` * `pipeline_task` * `power_bi_task` * `python_wheel_task` * `run_job_task` * `spark_jar_task` * `spark_python_task` * `spark_submit_task` * `sql_task`
databricks_job.alert_on_last_attempt: (Bool) do not send notifications to recipients specified in `on_start` for the retried runs and do not send notifications to recipients specified in `on_failure` until the last retry of the run
databricks_job.always_running: (Bool) Whenever the job is always running, like a Spark Streaming application, on every update restart the current active run or start it again, if nothing it is not running. False by default. Any job runs are started with `parameters` specified in `spark_jar_task` or `spark_submit_task` or `spark_python_task` or `notebook_task` blocks
databricks_job.base_parameters: (Map) Base parameters to be used for each run of this job. If the run is initiated by a call to run-now with parameters specified, the two parameters maps will be merged. If the same key is specified in base_parameters and in run-now, the value from run-now will be used. If the notebook takes a parameter that is not specified in the job's base_parameters or the run-now override parameters, the default value from the notebook will be used. Retrieve these parameters in a notebook using `dbutils.widgets.get`
databricks_job.branch: name of the Git branch to use. Conflicts with `tag` and `commit`
databricks_job.budget_policy_id: The ID of the user-specified budget policy to use for this job. If not specified, a default budget policy may be applied when creating or modifying the job
databricks_job.catalog: The name of the catalog to use inside Unity Catalog
databricks_job.commands: (Array) Series of dbt commands to execute in sequence. Every command must start with "dbt"
databricks_job.commit: hash of Git commit to use. Conflicts with `branch` and `tag`
databricks_job.compute: Task level compute configuration. This block is [documented below](#compute-configuration-block)
databricks_job.concurrency: Controls the number of active iteration task runs. Default is 20, maximum allowed is 100
databricks_job.condition: The table(s) condition based on which to trigger a job run.  Possible values are `ANY_UPDATED`, `ALL_UPDATED`
databricks_job.control_run_state: (Bool) If true, the Databricks provider will stop and start the job as needed to ensure that the active run for the job reflects the deployed configuration. For continuous jobs, the provider respects the `pause_status` by stopping the current active run. This flag cannot be set for non-continuous jobs
databricks_job.custom_subject: string specifying a custom subject of email sent
databricks_job.dashboard_id: (String) identifier of the Databricks SQL Dashboard [databricks_sql_dashboard](sql_dashboard.md)
databricks_job.dependencies: (list of strings) List of pip dependencies, as supported by the version of pip in this environment. Each dependency is a pip requirement file line.  See [API docs](https://docs.databricks.com/api/workspace/jobs/create#environments-spec-dependencies) for more information
databricks_job.depends_on: block specifying dependency(-ies) for a given task
databricks_job.disable_auto_optimization: A flag to disable auto optimization in serverless tasks
databricks_job.edit_mode: If `"UI_LOCKED"`, the user interface for the job will be locked. If `"EDITABLE"` (the default), the user interface will be editable
databricks_job.email_notifications: An optional block to specify a set of email addresses notified when this task begins, completes or fails. The default behavior is to not send any emails. This block is [documented below](#email_notifications-configuration-block)
databricks_job.enabled: If true, enable queueing for the job
databricks_job.entry_point: Python function as entry point for the task
databricks_job.environment_key: an unique identifier of the Environment.  It will be referenced from `environment_key` attribute of corresponding task
databricks_job.environment_version: client version used by the environment. Each version comes with a specific Python version and a set of Python packages
databricks_job.existing_cluster_id: Identifier of the [interactive cluster](cluster.md) to run job on.  *Note: running tasks on interactive clusters may lead to increased costs!*
databricks_job.file: block consisting of single string fields:
databricks_job.file_arrival: configuration block to define a trigger for [File Arrival events](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/workflows/jobs/file-arrival-triggers) consisting of following attributes:
databricks_job.full_refresh: (Bool) Specifies if there should be full refresh of the pipeline
databricks_job.git_source: Specifies the a Git repository for task source code. See [git_source Configuration Block](#git_source-configuration-block) below
databricks_job.hardware_accelerator: Hardware accelerator configuration for Serverless GPU workloads. Supported values are: * `GPU_1xA10`: GPU_1xA10: Single A10 GPU configuration. * `GPU_8xH100`: GPU_8xH100: 8x H100 GPU configuration
databricks_job.health: block described below that specifies health conditions for a given task
databricks_job.inputs: (String) Array for task to iterate on. This can be a JSON string or a reference to an array parameter
databricks_job.interval: Specifies the interval at which the job should run
databricks_job.job_cluster: A list of job [databricks_cluster](cluster.md) specifications that can be shared and reused by tasks of this job. Libraries cannot be declared in a shared job cluster. You must declare dependent libraries in task settings. *Multi-task syntax*
databricks_job.job_cluster_key: Identifier that can be referenced in `task` block, so that cluster is shared between tasks
databricks_job.left: The left operand of the condition task. It could be a string value, job state, or a parameter reference
databricks_job.library: (Set) An optional list of libraries to be installed on the cluster that will execute the job
databricks_job.main_class_name: The full name of the class containing the main method to be executed. This class must be contained in a JAR provided as a library. The code should use `SparkContext.getOrCreate` to obtain a Spark context; otherwise, runs of the job will fail
databricks_job.max_concurrent_runs: (Integer) An optional maximum allowed number of concurrent runs of the job. Defaults to *1*
databricks_job.max_retries: (Integer) An optional maximum number of times to retry an unsuccessful run. A run is considered to be unsuccessful if it completes with a `FAILED` or `INTERNAL_ERROR` lifecycle state. The value -1 means to retry indefinitely and the value 0 means to never retry. The default behavior is to never retry. A run can have the following lifecycle state: `PENDING`, `RUNNING`, `TERMINATING`, `TERMINATED`, `SKIPPED` or `INTERNAL_ERROR`
databricks_job.metric: string specifying the metric to check, like `RUN_DURATION_SECONDS`, `STREAMING_BACKLOG_FILES`, etc. - check the [Jobs REST API documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/api/workspace/jobs/create#health-rules-metric) for the full list of supported metrics
databricks_job.min_retry_interval_millis: (Integer) An optional minimal interval in milliseconds between the start of the failed run and the subsequent retry run. The default behavior is that unsuccessful runs are immediately retried
databricks_job.min_time_between_triggers_seconds: If set, the trigger starts a run only after the specified amount of time passed since the last time the trigger fired. The minimum allowed value is 60 seconds
databricks_job.name: The name of the defined parameter. May only contain alphanumeric characters, `_`, `-`, and `.`
databricks_job.new_cluster: Block with almost the same set of parameters as for [databricks_cluster](cluster.md) resource, except following (check the [REST API documentation for full list of supported parameters](https://docs.databricks.com/api/workspace/jobs/create#job_clusters-new_cluster)):
databricks_job.no_alert_for_canceled_runs: (Bool) don't send alert for cancelled runs
databricks_job.no_alert_for_skipped_runs: (Bool) don't send alert for skipped runs
databricks_job.notebook_path: The path of the [databricks_notebook](notebook.md#path) to be run in the Databricks workspace or remote repository. For notebooks stored in the Databricks workspace, the path must be absolute and begin with a slash. For notebooks stored in a remote repository, the path must be relative. This field is required
databricks_job.notification_settings: An optional block controlling the notification settings on the job level [documented below](#notification_settings-configuration-block)
databricks_job.on_duration_warning_threshold_exceeded: (List) list of notification IDs to call when the duration of a run exceeds the threshold specified by the `RUN_DURATION_SECONDS` metric in the `health` block
databricks_job.on_failure: (List) list of notification IDs to call when the run fails. A maximum of 3 destinations can be specified
databricks_job.on_start: (List) list of notification IDs to call when the run starts. A maximum of 3 destinations can be specified
databricks_job.on_streaming_backlog_exceeded: (List) list of notification IDs to call when any streaming backlog thresholds are exceeded for any stream
databricks_job.on_success: (List) list of notification IDs to call when the run completes successfully. A maximum of 3 destinations can be specified
databricks_job.op: string specifying the operation used to evaluate the given metric. The only supported operation is `GREATER_THAN`
databricks_job.outcome: Can only be specified on condition task dependencies. The outcome of the dependent task that must be met for this task to run. Possible values are `"true"` or `"false"`
databricks_job.parameter: Specifies job parameter for the job. See [parameter Configuration Block](#parameter-configuration-block)
databricks_job.parameters: (Map) parameters to be used for each run of this task. The SQL alert task does not support custom parameters
databricks_job.path: If `source` is `GIT`: Relative path to the file in the repository specified in the `git_source` block with SQL commands to execute. If `source` is `WORKSPACE`: Absolute path to the file in the workspace with SQL commands to execute
databricks_job.pause_status: Indicate whether this trigger is paused or not. Either `PAUSED` or `UNPAUSED`. When the `pause_status` field is omitted in the block, the server will default to using `UNPAUSED` as a value for `pause_status`
databricks_job.pause_subscriptions: flag that specifies if subscriptions are paused or not
databricks_job.performance_target: The performance mode on a serverless job. The performance target determines the level of compute performance or cost-efficiency for the run.  Supported values are: * `PERFORMANCE_OPTIMIZED`: (default value) Prioritizes fast startup and execution times through rapid scaling and optimized cluster performance. * `STANDARD`: Enables cost-efficient execution of serverless workloads
databricks_job.periodic: configuration block to define a trigger for Periodic Triggers consisting of the following attributes:
databricks_job.profiles_directory: The relative path to the directory in the repository specified by `git_source` where dbt should look in for the `profiles.yml` file. If not specified, defaults to the repository's root directory. Equivalent to passing `--profile-dir` to a dbt command
databricks_job.project_directory: The path where dbt should look for `dbt_project.yml`. Equivalent to passing `--project-dir` to the dbt CLI. * If `source` is `GIT`: Relative path to the directory in the repository specified in the `git_source` block. Defaults to the repository's root directory when not specified. * If `source` is `WORKSPACE`: Absolute path to the folder in the workspace
databricks_job.provider: case insensitive name of the Git provider.  Following values are supported right now (could be a subject for change, consult [Repos API documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/dev-tools/api/latest/repos.html)): `gitHub`, `gitHubEnterprise`, `bitbucketCloud`, `bitbucketServer`, `azureDevOpsServices`, `gitLab`, `gitLabEnterpriseEdition`
databricks_job.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_job.python_file: The URI of the Python file to be executed. Cloud file URIs (e.g. `s3:/`, `abfss:/`, `gs:/`), workspace paths and remote repository are supported. For Python files stored in the Databricks workspace, the path must be absolute and begin with `/`. For files stored in a remote repository, the path must be relative. This field is required
databricks_job.quartz_cron_expression: A [Cron expression using Quartz syntax](http://www.quartz-scheduler.org/documentation/quartz-2.3.0/tutorials/crontrigger.html) that describes the schedule for a job. This field is required
databricks_job.query: block consisting of single string field: `query_id` - identifier of the Databricks Query ([databricks_query](query.md))
databricks_job.queue: The queue status for the job. See [queue Configuration Block](#queue-configuration-block) below
databricks_job.retry_on_timeout: (Bool) An optional policy to specify whether to retry a job when it times out. The default behavior is to not retry on timeout
databricks_job.right: The right operand of the condition task. It could be a string value, job state, or parameter reference
databricks_job.rules: (List) list of rules that are represented as objects with the following attributes:
databricks_job.run_as: The user or the service principal the job runs as. See [run_as Configuration Block](#run_as-configuration-block) below
databricks_job.run_if: An optional value indicating the condition that determines whether the task should be run once its dependencies have been completed. One of `ALL_SUCCESS`, `AT_LEAST_ONE_SUCCESS`, `NONE_FAILED`, `ALL_DONE`, `AT_LEAST_ONE_FAILED` or `ALL_FAILED`. When omitted, defaults to `ALL_SUCCESS`
databricks_job.schedule: An optional periodic schedule for this job. The default behavior is that the job runs when triggered by clicking Run Now in the Jobs UI or sending an API request to runNow. See [schedule Configuration Block](#schedule-configuration-block) below
databricks_job.schema: The name of the schema dbt should run in. Defaults to `default`
databricks_job.service_principal_name: The application ID of an active service principal. Setting this field requires the `servicePrincipal/user` role
databricks_job.source: The source of the project. Possible values are `WORKSPACE` and `GIT`
databricks_job.spec: block describing the Environment. Consists of following attributes:
databricks_job.subscriptions: a list of subscription blocks consisting out of one of the required fields: `user_name` for user emails or `destination_id` - for Alert destination's identifier
databricks_job.table_names: A non-empty list of tables to monitor for changes. The table name must be in the format `catalog_name.schema_name.table_name`
databricks_job.table_update: configuration block to define a trigger for [Table Updates](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/jobs/trigger-table-update) consisting of following attributes:
databricks_job.tag: name of the Git branch to use. Conflicts with `branch` and `commit`
databricks_job.tags: An optional map of the tags associated with the job. See [tags Configuration Map](#tags-configuration-map)
databricks_job.task: Task to run against the `inputs` list
databricks_job.task_key: The name of the task this task depends on
databricks_job.task_retry_mode: Controls task level retry behaviour. Allowed values are: * `NEVER` (default): The failed task will not be retried. * `ON_FAILURE`: Retry a failed task if at least one other task in the job is still running its first attempt. When this condition is no longer met or the retry limit is reached, the job run is cancelled and a new run is started
databricks_job.timeout_seconds: (Integer) An optional timeout applied to each run of this job. The default behavior is to have no timeout
databricks_job.timezone_id: A Java timezone ID. The schedule for a job will be resolved with respect to this timezone. See Java TimeZone for details. This field is required
databricks_job.trigger: The conditions that triggers the job to start. See [trigger Configuration Block](#trigger-configuration-block) below. * `continuous`- (Optional) Configuration block to configure pause status. See [continuous Configuration Block](#continuous-configuration-block)
databricks_job.unit: The unit of time for the interval.  Possible values are: `DAYS`, `HOURS`, `WEEKS`
databricks_job.url: URL of the job on the given workspace
databricks_job.user_name: The email of an active workspace user. Non-admin users can only set this field to their own email
databricks_job.value: integer value used to compare to the given metric
databricks_job.wait_after_last_change_seconds: If set, the trigger starts a run only after no file activity has occurred for the specified amount of time. This makes it possible to wait for a batch of incoming files to arrive before triggering a run. The minimum allowed value is 60 seconds
databricks_job.warehouse_id: ID of the (the [databricks_sql_endpoint](sql_endpoint.md)) that will be used to execute the task.  Only Serverless & Pro warehouses are supported right now
databricks_job.webhook_notifications: (List) An optional set of system destinations (for example, webhook destinations or Slack) to be notified when runs of this task begins, completes or fails. The default behavior is to not send any notifications. This field is a block and is documented below
databricks_job.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_library.cluster_id: ID of the [databricks_cluster](cluster.md) to install the library on
databricks_library.cran: Configuration block for a CRAN library. The block consists of the following fields:
databricks_library.egg: Path to the EGG library. Installing Python egg files is deprecated and is not supported in Databricks Runtime 14.0 and above. Use `whl` or `pypi` instead
databricks_library.exclusions: List of dependencies to exclude. For example: `["slf4j:slf4j", "*:hadoop-client"]`. See [Maven dependency exclusions](https://maven.apache.org/guides/introduction/introduction-to-optional-and-excludes-dependencies.html) for more information
databricks_library.jar: Path to the JAR library. Supported URIs include Workspace paths, Unity Catalog Volumes paths, and S3 URIs. For example: `/Workspace/path/to/library.jar`, `/Volumes/path/to/library.jar` or `s3://my-bucket/library.jar`. If S3 is used, make sure the cluster has read access to the library. You may need to launch the cluster with an IAM role to access the S3 URI
databricks_library.maven: Configuration block for a Maven library. The block consists of the following fields:
databricks_library.package: The name of the CRAN package to install
databricks_library.provider_config: Configuration block for management through the account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_library.pypi: Configuration block for a PyPI library. The block consists of the following fields:
databricks_library.repo: The repository where the package can be found. If not specified, the default CRAN repo is used
databricks_library.requirements: Path to the requirements.txt file. Only Workspace paths and Unity Catalog Volumes paths are supported. For example: `/Workspace/path/to/requirements.txt` or `/Volumes/path/to/requirements.txt`. Requires a cluster with DBR 15.0+
databricks_library.whl: Path to the wheel library. Supported URIs include Workspace paths, Unity Catalog Volumes paths, and S3 URIs. For example: `/Workspace/path/to/library.whl`, `/Volumes/path/to/library.whl` or `s3://my-bucket/library.whl`. If S3 is used, make sure the cluster has read access to the library. You may need to launch the cluster with an IAM role to access the S3 URI
databricks_library.workspace_id: Workspace ID that the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account that the provider is configured with

databricks_metastore.cloud: Cloud vendor of the metastore home shard (e.g., `aws`, `azure`, `gcp`).
databricks_metastore.created_at: Time at which this metastore was created, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_metastore.default_data_access_config_id: Unique identifier of the metastore's (Default) Data Access Configuration.
databricks_metastore.delta_sharing_organization_name: The organization name of a Delta Sharing entity, to be used in Databricks-to-Databricks Delta Sharing as the official name.
databricks_metastore.delta_sharing_recipient_token_lifetime_i: The lifetime of delta sharing recipient token in seconds.
databricks_metastore.delta_sharing_scope: The scope of Delta Sharing enabled for the metastore.
databricks_metastore.external_access_enabled: Whether to allow non-DBR clients to directly access entities under the metastore.
databricks_metastore.global_metastore_id: Globally unique metastore ID across clouds and regions, of the form `cloud:region:metastore_id`.
databricks_metastore.name: The user-specified name of the metastore.
databricks_metastore.privilege_model_version: Privilege model version of the metastore, of the form `major.minor` (e.g., `1.0`).
databricks_metastore.region: Cloud region which the metastore serves (e.g., `us-west-2`, `westus`).
databricks_metastore.storage_root: The storage root URL for metastore
databricks_metastore.storage_root_credential_id: UUID of storage credential to access the metastore storage_root.
databricks_metastore.storage_root_credential_name: Name of the storage credential to access the metastore storage_root.
databricks_metastore.updated_at: Time at which the metastore was last modified, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_metastore.updated_by: Username of user who last modified the metastore.

databricks_metastore_assignment.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_metastore_assignment.default_catalog_name: (Deprecated) Default catalog used for this assignment. Please use [databricks_default_namespace_setting](default_namespace_setting.md) instead
databricks_metastore_assignment.id: ID of this metastore assignment in form of `<workspace_id>|<metastore_id>`
databricks_metastore_assignment.metastore_id: Unique identifier of the parent Metastore
databricks_metastore_assignment.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_metastore_assignment.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_metastore_data_access.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_metastore_data_access.id: ID of this data access configuration in form of `<metastore_id>|<name>`
databricks_metastore_data_access.is_default: whether to set this credential as the default for the metastore. In practice, this should always be true
databricks_metastore_data_access.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_metastore_data_access.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_mlflow_experiment.artifact_location: Path to artifact location of the MLflow experiment
databricks_mlflow_experiment.name: Name of MLflow experiment. It must be an absolute path within the Databricks workspace, e.g. `/Users/<some-username>/my-experiment`. For more information about changes to experiment naming conventions, see [mlflow docs](https://docs.databricks.com/applications/mlflow/experiments.html#experiment-migration)
databricks_mlflow_experiment.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_mlflow_experiment.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_mlflow_model.description: The description of the MLflow model
databricks_mlflow_model.id: ID of the MLflow model, the same as `name`
databricks_mlflow_model.name: Name of MLflow model. Change of name triggers new resource
databricks_mlflow_model.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_mlflow_model.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_mlflow_webhook.access_token: The personal access token used to authorize webhook's job runs
databricks_mlflow_webhook.authorization: Value of the authorization header that should be sent in the request sent by the wehbook.  It should be of the form `<auth type> <credentials>`, e.g. `Bearer <access_token>`. If set to an empty string, no authorization header will be included in the request
databricks_mlflow_webhook.description: Optional description of the MLflow webhook
databricks_mlflow_webhook.enable_ssl_verification: Enable/disable SSL certificate validation. Default is `true`. For self-signed certificates, this field must be `false` AND the destination server must disable certificate validation as well. For security purposes, it is encouraged to perform secret validation with the HMAC-encoded portion of the payload and acknowledge the risk associated with disabling hostname validation whereby it becomes more likely that requests can be maliciously routed to an unintended host
databricks_mlflow_webhook.events: The list of events that will trigger execution of Databricks job or POSTing to an URL, for example, `MODEL_VERSION_CREATED`, `MODEL_VERSION_TRANSITIONED_STAGE`, `TRANSITION_REQUEST_CREATED`, etc.  Refer to the [Webhooks API documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/dev-tools/api/latest/mlflow.html#operation/create-registry-webhook) for a full list of supported events
databricks_mlflow_webhook.id: Unique ID of the MLflow Webhook
databricks_mlflow_webhook.job_id: ID of the Databricks job that the webhook runs
databricks_mlflow_webhook.model_name: Name of MLflow model for which webhook will be created. If the model name is not specified, a registry-wide webhook is created that listens for the specified events across all versions of all registered models
databricks_mlflow_webhook.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_mlflow_webhook.secret: Shared secret required for HMAC encoding payload. The HMAC-encoded payload will be sent in the header as `X-Databricks-Signature: encoded_payload`
databricks_mlflow_webhook.status: Optional status of webhook. Possible values are `ACTIVE`, `TEST_MODE`, `DISABLED`. Default is `ACTIVE`
databricks_mlflow_webhook.url: External HTTPS URL called on event trigger (by using a POST request). Structure of payload depends on the event type, refer to [documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/applications/mlflow/model-registry-webhooks.html) for more details
databricks_mlflow_webhook.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with
databricks_mlflow_webhook.workspace_url: URL of the workspace containing the job that this webhook runs. If not specified, the job’s workspace URL is assumed to be the same as the workspace where the webhook is created

databricks_model_serving.ai21labs_api_key: The Databricks secret key reference for an AI21Labs API key
databricks_model_serving.ai21labs_api_key_plaintext: An AI21 Labs API key provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.ai_gateway: A block with AI Gateway configuration for the serving endpoint. *Note: only external model endpoints are supported as of now.*
databricks_model_serving.anthropic_api_key: The Databricks secret key reference for an Anthropic API key
databricks_model_serving.anthropic_api_key_plaintext: The Anthropic API key provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.api_key_auth: API key authentication for the custom provider API. Conflicts with `bearer_token_auth`. * `key` (Required) - The name of the API key parameter used for authentication. * `value` (Optional) - The Databricks secret key reference for an API Key. * `value_plaintext` (Optional) - The API Key provided as a plaintext string. * `bearer_token_auth` (Optional) - bearer token authentication for the custom provider API.  Conflicts with `api_key_auth`. * `token` (Optional) -  The Databricks secret key reference for a token. * `token_plaintext` (Optional) - The token provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.auto_capture_config: Configuration for Inference Tables which automatically logs requests and responses to Unity Catalog
databricks_model_serving.aws_access_key_id: The Databricks secret key reference for an AWS Access Key ID with permissions to interact with Bedrock services
databricks_model_serving.aws_access_key_id_plaintext: An AWS access key ID with permissions to interact with Bedrock services provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.aws_region: The AWS region to use. Bedrock has to be enabled there
databricks_model_serving.aws_secret_access_key: The Databricks secret key reference for an AWS Secret Access Key paired with the access key ID, with permissions to interact with Bedrock services
databricks_model_serving.aws_secret_access_key_plaintext: An AWS secret access key paired with the access key ID, with permissions to interact with Bedrock services provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.bedrock_provider: The underlying provider in Amazon Bedrock. Supported values (case insensitive) include: `Anthropic`, `Cohere`, `AI21Labs`, `Amazon`
databricks_model_serving.behavior: a string that describes the behavior for PII filter. Currently only `BLOCK` value is supported
databricks_model_serving.budget_policy_id: (Optiona) The Budget Policy ID set for this serving endpoint
databricks_model_serving.calls: Used to specify how many calls are allowed for a key within the renewal_period
databricks_model_serving.catalog_name: The name of the catalog in Unity Catalog. NOTE: On update, you cannot change the catalog name if it was already set
databricks_model_serving.cohere_api_key: The Databricks secret key reference for a Cohere API key
databricks_model_serving.cohere_api_key_plaintext: The Cohere API key provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.config: The config for the external model, which must match the provider. *Note that API keys could be provided either as a reference to the Databricks Secret (parameters without `_plaintext` suffix) or in plain text (parameters with `_plaintext` suffix)!*
databricks_model_serving.custom_provider_config: Custom Provider Config. Only required if the provider is 'custom'. * `custom_provider_url` (Required) - URL of the custom provider API
databricks_model_serving.databricks_api_token: The Databricks secret key reference for a Databricks API token that corresponds to a user or service principal with Can Query access to the model serving endpoint pointed to by this external model
databricks_model_serving.databricks_api_token_plaintext: The Databricks API token that corresponds to a user or service principal with Can Query access to the model serving endpoint pointed to by this external model provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.databricks_workspace_url: The URL of the Databricks workspace containing the model serving endpoint pointed to by this external model
databricks_model_serving.description: The description of the model serving endpoint
databricks_model_serving.email_notifications: A block with Email notification setting
databricks_model_serving.enabled: boolean flag specifying if usage tracking is enabled
databricks_model_serving.entity_name: The name of the entity to be served. The entity may be a model in the Databricks Model Registry, a model in the Unity Catalog (UC), or a function of type `FEATURE_SPEC` in the UC. If it is a UC object, the full name of the object should be given in the form of `catalog_name.schema_name.model_name`
databricks_model_serving.entity_version: The version of the model in Databricks Model Registry to be served or empty if the entity is a `FEATURE_SPEC`
databricks_model_serving.environment_vars: a map of environment variable names/values that will be used for serving this model.  Environment variables may refer to Databricks secrets using the standard syntax: `{{secrets/secret_scope/secret_key}}`
databricks_model_serving.external_model: The external model to be served. NOTE: Only one of `external_model` and (`entity_name`, `entity_version`, `workload_size`, `workload_type`, and `scale_to_zero_enabled`) can be specified with the latter set being used for custom model serving for a Databricks registered model. When an `external_model` is present, the served entities list can only have one `served_entity` object. An existing endpoint with `external_model` can not be updated to an endpoint without `external_model`. If the endpoint is created without `external_model`, users cannot update it to add `external_model` later
databricks_model_serving.fallback_config: block with configuration for traffic fallback which auto fallbacks to other served entities if the request to a served entity fails with certain error codes, to increase availability
databricks_model_serving.guardrails: Block with configuration for AI Guardrails to prevent unwanted data and unsafe data in requests and responses. Consists of the following attributes:
databricks_model_serving.id: Equal to the `name` argument and used to identify the serving endpoint
databricks_model_serving.inference_table_config: Block describing the configuration of usage tracking. Consists of the following attributes:
databricks_model_serving.input: A block with configuration for input guardrail filters:
databricks_model_serving.instance_profile_arn: ARN of the instance profile that the served model will use to access AWS resources
databricks_model_serving.invalid_keywords: (Deprecated) List of invalid keywords. AI guardrail uses keyword or string matching to decide if the keyword exists in the request or response content
databricks_model_serving.key: Key field for a serving endpoint rate limit. Currently, `user`, `user_group`, `service_principal`, and `endpoint` are supported, with `endpoint` being the default if not specified
databricks_model_serving.max_provisioned_concurrency: The maximum provisioned concurrency that the endpoint can scale up to. Conflicts with `workload_size`
databricks_model_serving.max_provisioned_throughput: The maximum tokens per second that the endpoint can scale up to
databricks_model_serving.microsoft_entra_client_id: This field is only required for Azure AD OpenAI and is the Microsoft Entra Client ID
databricks_model_serving.microsoft_entra_client_secret: The Databricks secret key reference for a client secret used for Microsoft Entra ID authentication
databricks_model_serving.microsoft_entra_client_secret_plaintext: The client secret used for Microsoft Entra ID authentication provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.microsoft_entra_tenant_id: This field is only required for Azure AD OpenAI and is the Microsoft Entra Tenant ID
databricks_model_serving.min_provisioned_concurrency: The minimum provisioned concurrency that the endpoint can scale down to. Conflicts with `workload_size`
databricks_model_serving.min_provisioned_throughput: The minimum tokens per second that the endpoint can scale down to
databricks_model_serving.model_name: The name of the model in Databricks Model Registry to be served
databricks_model_serving.model_version: The version of the model in Databricks Model Registry to be served
databricks_model_serving.name: The name of a served model. It must be unique across an endpoint. If not specified, this field will default to `modelname-modelversion`. A served model name can consist of alphanumeric characters, dashes, and underscores
databricks_model_serving.on_update_failure: a list of email addresses to be notified when an endpoint fails to update its configuration or state
databricks_model_serving.on_update_success: a list of email addresses to be notified when an endpoint successfully updates its configuration or state
databricks_model_serving.openai_api_base: This is the base URL for the OpenAI API (default: "<https://api.openai.com/v1>"). For Azure OpenAI, this field is required and is the base URL for the Azure OpenAI API service provided by Azure
databricks_model_serving.openai_api_key: The Databricks secret key reference for an OpenAI or Azure OpenAI API key
databricks_model_serving.openai_api_key_plaintext: The OpenAI API key using the OpenAI or Azure service provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.openai_api_type: This is an optional field to specify the type of OpenAI API to use. For Azure OpenAI, this field is required, and this parameter represents the preferred security access validation protocol. For access token validation, use `azure`. For authentication using Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) use, `azuread`
databricks_model_serving.openai_api_version: This is an optional field to specify the OpenAI API version. For Azure OpenAI, this field is required and is the version of the Azure OpenAI service to utilize, specified by a date
databricks_model_serving.openai_deployment_name: This field is only required for Azure OpenAI and is the name of the deployment resource for the Azure OpenAI service
databricks_model_serving.openai_organization: This is an optional field to specify the organization in OpenAI or Azure OpenAI
databricks_model_serving.output: A block with configuration for output guardrail filters.  Has the same structure as `input` block
databricks_model_serving.palm_api_key: The Databricks secret key reference for a PaLM API key
databricks_model_serving.palm_api_key_plaintext: The PaLM API key provided as a plaintext string
databricks_model_serving.pii: Block with configuration for guardrail PII filter:
databricks_model_serving.principal: Principal field for a user, user group, or service principal to apply rate limiting to. Accepts a user email, group name, or service principal application ID
databricks_model_serving.private_key: The Databricks secret key reference for a private key for the service account that has access to the Google Cloud Vertex AI Service
databricks_model_serving.private_key_plaintext: The private key for the service account that has access to the Google Cloud Vertex AI Service is provided as a plaintext secret
databricks_model_serving.project_id: This is the Google Cloud project id that the service account is associated with
databricks_model_serving.provider: The name of the provider for the external model. Currently, the supported providers are `ai21labs`, `anthropic`, `amazon-bedrock`, `cohere`, `databricks-model-serving`, `google-cloud-vertex-ai`, `openai`, and `palm`
databricks_model_serving.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_model_serving.rate_limits: Block describing rate limits for AI gateway. For details see the description of `rate_limits` block above
databricks_model_serving.region: This is the region for the Google Cloud Vertex AI Service
databricks_model_serving.renewal_period: Renewal period field for a serving endpoint rate limit. Currently, only `minute` is supported
databricks_model_serving.route_optimized: A boolean enabling route optimization for the endpoint. *Note: only available for custom models.*
databricks_model_serving.routes: Each block represents a route that defines traffic to each served entity. Each `served_entity` block needs to have a corresponding `routes` block
databricks_model_serving.safety: the boolean flag that indicates whether the safety filter is enabled
databricks_model_serving.scale_to_zero_enabled: Whether the compute resources for the served model should scale down to zero. If `scale-to-zero` is enabled, the lower bound of the provisioned concurrency for each workload size will be 0. The default value is `true`
databricks_model_serving.schema_name: The name of the schema in Unity Catalog. NOTE: On update, you cannot change the schema name if it was already set
databricks_model_serving.served_entities: A list of served entities for the endpoint to serve. A serving endpoint can have up to 10 served entities
databricks_model_serving.served_entity_name: The name of the served entity this route configures traffic for. This needs to match the name of a `served_entity` block
databricks_model_serving.served_models: (Deprecated, use `served_entities` instead) Each block represents a served model for the endpoint to serve. A model serving endpoint can have up to 10 served models
databricks_model_serving.serving_endpoint_id: Unique identifier of the serving endpoint primarily used to set permissions and refer to this instance for other operations
databricks_model_serving.table_name_prefix: The prefix of the table in Unity Catalog. NOTE: On update, you cannot change the prefix name if it was already set
