databricks_model_serving.tags: Tags to be attached to the serving endpoint and automatically propagated to billing logs
databricks_model_serving.task: The task type of the external model
databricks_model_serving.tokens: Specifies how many tokens are allowed for a key within the renewal_period
databricks_model_serving.traffic_config: A single block represents the traffic split configuration amongst the served models
databricks_model_serving.traffic_percentage: The percentage of endpoint traffic to send to this route. It must be an integer between 0 and 100 inclusive
databricks_model_serving.usage_tracking_config: Block with configuration for payload logging using inference tables. For details see the description of `auto_capture_config` block above
databricks_model_serving.valid_topics: (Deprecated) The list of allowed topics. Given a chat request, this guardrail flags the request if its topic is not in the allowed topics
databricks_model_serving.value: The value field for a tag
databricks_model_serving.workload_size: The workload size of the served model. The workload size corresponds to a range of provisioned concurrency that the compute will autoscale between. A single unit of provisioned concurrency can process one request at a time. Valid workload sizes are `Small` (4 - 4 provisioned concurrency), `Medium` (8 - 16 provisioned concurrency), and `Large` (16 - 64 provisioned concurrency)
databricks_model_serving.workload_type: The workload type of the served model. The workload type selects which type of compute to use in the endpoint. For deep learning workloads, GPU acceleration is available by selecting workload types like `GPU_SMALL` and others. See the documentation for all options. The default value is `CPU`
databricks_model_serving.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_mws_ncc_binding.network_connectivity_config_id: Canonical unique identifier of Network Connectivity Config in Databricks Account
databricks_mws_ncc_binding.workspace_id: Identifier of the workspace to attach the NCC to. Change forces creation of a new resource

databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.cidr_blocks: list of IP CIDR blocks. * `azure_service_endpoint_rule` (Azure only) - block with information about stable Azure service endpoints. You can configure the firewall of your Azure resources to allow traffic from your Databricks serverless compute resources.  Consists of the following fields:
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.creation_time: time in epoch milliseconds when this object was created
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.default_rules: block describing network connectivity rules that are applied by default without resource specific configurations.  Consists of the following fields: * `aws_stable_ip_rule` (AWS only) - block with information about stable AWS IP CIDR blocks. You can use these to configure the firewall of your resources to allow traffic from your Databricks workspace.  Consists of the following fields:
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.egress_config: block containing information about network connectivity rules that apply to network traffic from your serverless compute resources. Consists of the following fields:
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.id: combination of `account_id` and `network_connectivity_config_id` separated by `/` character
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.name: Name of the network connectivity configuration. The name can contain alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores. The length must be between 3 and 30 characters. The name must match the regular expression `^[0-9a-zA-Z-_]{3,30}$`. Change forces creation of a new resource
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.network_connectivity_config_id: Canonical unique identifier of Network Connectivity Config in Databricks Account
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.region: Region of the Network Connectivity Config. NCCs can only be referenced by your workspaces in the same region. Change forces creation of a new resource
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.subnets: list of subnets from which Databricks network traffic originates when accessing your Azure resources
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.target_region: the Azure region in which this service endpoint rule applies
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.target_rules: block describing network connectivity rules that configured for each destinations. These rules override default rules.  Consists of the following fields: * `azure_private_endpoint_rules` (Azure only) - list containing information about configure Azure Private Endpoints. * `aws_private_endpoint_rules` (AWS only) - list containing information about configure AWS Private Endpoints
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.target_services: the Azure services to which this service endpoint rule applies to
databricks_mws_network_connectivity_conf.updated_time: time in epoch milliseconds when this object was updated

databricks_mws_permission_assignment.\"ADMIN\": Adds principal to the workspace `admins` group. This gives workspace admin privileges to manage users and groups, workspace configurations, and more
databricks_mws_permission_assignment.\"USER\": Adds principal to the workspace `users` group. This gives basic workspace access
databricks_mws_permission_assignment.id: ID of the permission assignment in form of `workspace_id|principal_id`
databricks_mws_permission_assignment.permissions: The list of workspace permissions to assign to the principal:
databricks_mws_permission_assignment.principal_id: Databricks ID of the user, service principal, or group. The principal ID can be retrieved using the SCIM API, or using [databricks_user](../data-sources/user.md), [databricks_service_principal](../data-sources/service_principal.md) or [databricks_group](../data-sources/group.md) data sources

databricks_notebook.content_base64: The base64-encoded notebook source code. Conflicts with `source`. Use of `content_base64` is discouraged, as it's increasing memory footprint of Terraform state and should only be used in exceptional circumstances, like creating a notebook with configuration properties for a data pipeline
databricks_notebook.path: The absolute path of the notebook or directory, beginning with "/", e.g. "/Demo"
databricks_notebook.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_notebook.source: Path to notebook in source code format on local filesystem. Conflicts with `content_base64`
databricks_notebook.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with
databricks_notebook.workspace_path: path on Workspace File System (WSFS) in form of `/Workspace` + `path`

databricks_notification_destination.addresses: The list of email addresses to send notifications to
databricks_notification_destination.app_id: App ID for Microsoft Teams App
databricks_notification_destination.auth_secret: Secret for Microsoft Teams App authentication
databricks_notification_destination.channel_url: Channel URL for Microsoft Teams App
databricks_notification_destination.config: The configuration of the Notification Destination. It must contain exactly one of the following blocks:
databricks_notification_destination.display_name: The display name of the Notification Destination
databricks_notification_destination.email: The email configuration of the Notification Destination. It must contain the following:
databricks_notification_destination.generic_webhook: The Generic Webhook configuration of the Notification Destination. It must contain the following:
databricks_notification_destination.id: The unique ID of the Notification Destination
databricks_notification_destination.microsoft_teams: The Microsoft Teams configuration of the Notification Destination. It must contain the following:
databricks_notification_destination.pagerduty: The PagerDuty configuration of the Notification Destination. It must contain the following:
databricks_notification_destination.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_notification_destination.slack: The Slack configuration of the Notification Destination. It must contain the following:
databricks_notification_destination.tenant_id: Tenant ID for Microsoft Teams App
databricks_notification_destination.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_obo_token.application_id: Application ID of [databricks_service_principal](service_principal.md#application_id) to create a PAT token for
databricks_obo_token.comment: (String, Optional) Comment that describes the purpose of the token
databricks_obo_token.id: Canonical unique identifier for the token
databricks_obo_token.lifetime_seconds: (Integer, Optional) The number of seconds before the token expires. Token resource is re-created when it expires. If no lifetime is specified, the token remains valid indefinitely
databricks_obo_token.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_obo_token.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_online_table.detailed_state: The state of the online table
databricks_online_table.id: The same as the name of the online table. * `spec`:
databricks_online_table.message: A text description of the current state of the online table
databricks_online_table.name: 3-level name of the Online Table to create
databricks_online_table.perform_full_copy: Whether to create a full-copy pipeline -- a pipeline that stops after creates a full copy of the source table upon initialization and does not process any change data feeds (CDFs) afterwards. The pipeline can still be manually triggered afterwards, but it always perform a full copy of the source table and there are no incremental updates. This mode is useful for syncing views or tables without CDFs to online tables. Note that the full-copy pipeline only supports "triggered" scheduling policy
databricks_online_table.pipeline_id: ID of the associated Delta Live Table pipeline
databricks_online_table.primary_key_columns: list of the columns comprising the primary key
databricks_online_table.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_online_table.run_continuously: empty block that specifies that pipeline runs continuously after generating the initial data.  Conflicts with `run_triggered`
databricks_online_table.run_triggered: empty block that specifies that pipeline stops after generating the initial data and can be triggered later (manually, through a cron job or through data triggers)
databricks_online_table.source_table_full_name: full name of the source table
databricks_online_table.spec: object containing specification of the online table:
databricks_online_table.status: object describing status of the online table:
databricks_online_table.table_serving_url: Data serving REST API URL for this table
databricks_online_table.timeseries_key: Time series key to deduplicate (tie-break) rows with the same primary key
databricks_online_table.unity_catalog_provisioning_state: The provisioning state of the online table entity in Unity Catalog. This is distinct from the state of the data synchronization pipeline (i.e. the table may be in "ACTIVE" but the pipeline may be in "PROVISIONING" as it runs asynchronously)
databricks_online_table.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_permissions.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_permissions.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_pipeline.allow_duplicate_names: Optional boolean flag. If false, deployment will fail if name conflicts with that of another pipeline. default is `false`
databricks_pipeline.budget_policy_id: optional string specifying ID of the budget policy for this Lakeflow Declarative Pipeline
databricks_pipeline.catalog: The UC catalog the event log is published under
databricks_pipeline.channel: optional name of the release channel for Spark version used by Lakeflow Declarative Pipeline.  Supported values are: `CURRENT` (default) and `PREVIEW`
databricks_pipeline.configuration: An optional list of values to apply to the entire pipeline. Elements must be formatted as key:value pairs. * `library` blocks - Specifies pipeline code
databricks_pipeline.connection_id: Deprecated, Immutable. The Unity Catalog connection this gateway pipeline uses to communicate with the source. *Use `connection_name` instead!*
databricks_pipeline.connection_name: Immutable. The Unity Catalog connection this ingestion pipeline uses to communicate with the source. Specify either ingestion_gateway_id or connection_name
databricks_pipeline.continuous: A flag indicating whether to run the pipeline continuously. The default value is `false`
databricks_pipeline.dependencies: a list of pip dependencies, as supported by the version of pip in this environment. Each dependency is a [pip requirement file line](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/reference/requirements-file-format/).  See [API docs](https://docs.databricks.com/api/azure/workspace/pipelines/create#environment-dependencies) for more information
databricks_pipeline.deployment: Deployment type of this pipeline. Supports following attributes:
databricks_pipeline.development: A flag indicating whether to run the pipeline in development mode. The default value is `false`
databricks_pipeline.edition: optional name of the [product edition](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dlt/configure-pipeline#choose-a-product-edition). Supported values are: `CORE`, `PRO`, `ADVANCED` (default).  Not required when `serverless` is set to `true`
databricks_pipeline.event_log: an optional block specifying a table where LDP Event Log will be stored.  Consists of the following fields:
databricks_pipeline.file: specifies path to a file in Databricks Workspace to include as source. Actual path is specified as `path` attribute inside the block
databricks_pipeline.filters: Filters on which Pipeline packages to include in the deployed graph.  This block consists of following attributes:
databricks_pipeline.gateway_definition: The definition of a gateway pipeline to support CDC. Consists of following attributes:
databricks_pipeline.gateway_storage_catalog: Required, Immutable. The name of the catalog for the gateway pipeline's storage location
databricks_pipeline.gateway_storage_name: Required. The Unity Catalog-compatible naming for the gateway storage location. This is the destination to use for the data that is extracted by the gateway. Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines system will automatically create the storage location under the catalog and schema
databricks_pipeline.gateway_storage_schema: Required, Immutable. The name of the schema for the gateway pipelines's storage location
databricks_pipeline.glob: The unified field to include source code. Each entry should have the `include` attribute that can specify a notebook path, a file path, or a folder path that ends `/**` (to include everything from that folder). This field cannot be used together with `notebook` or `file`
databricks_pipeline.id: Canonical unique identifier of the Lakeflow Declarative Pipeline
databricks_pipeline.ingestion_gateway_id: Immutable. Identifier for the ingestion gateway used by this ingestion pipeline to communicate with the source. Specify either ingestion_gateway_id or connection_name
databricks_pipeline.kind: The deployment method that manages the pipeline
databricks_pipeline.metadata_file_path: The path to the file containing metadata about the deployment
databricks_pipeline.name: The table name the event log is published to in UC
databricks_pipeline.notebook: specifies path to a Databricks Notebook to include as source. Actual path is specified as `path` attribute inside the block
databricks_pipeline.objects: Required. Settings specifying tables to replicate and the destination for the replicated tables
databricks_pipeline.on-update-failure: a pipeline update fails with a retryable error
databricks_pipeline.on-update-fatal-failure: a pipeline update fails with a non-retryable (fatal) error
databricks_pipeline.photon: A flag indicating whether to use Photon engine. The default value is `false`
databricks_pipeline.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_pipeline.root_path: An optional string specifying the root path for this pipeline. This is used as the root directory when editing the pipeline in the Databricks user interface and it is added to `sys.path` when executing Python sources during pipeline execution. * `cluster` blocks - [Clusters](cluster.md) to run the pipeline. If none is specified, pipelines will automatically select a default cluster configuration for the pipeline. *Please note that Lakeflow Declarative Pipeline clusters are supporting only subset of attributes as described in [documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/api/workspace/pipelines/create#clusters).*  Also, note that `autoscale` block is extended with the `mode` parameter that controls the autoscaling algorithm (possible values are `ENHANCED` for new, enhanced autoscaling algorithm, or `LEGACY` for old algorithm)
databricks_pipeline.run_as: The user or the service principal the pipeline runs as. See [run_as Configuration Block](#run_as-configuration-block) below
databricks_pipeline.schema: The UC schema the event log is published under
databricks_pipeline.serverless: An optional flag indicating if serverless compute should be used for this Lakeflow Declarative Pipeline.  Requires `catalog` to be set, as it could be used only with Unity Catalog
databricks_pipeline.service_principal_name: The application ID of an active service principal. Setting this field requires the `servicePrincipal/user` role
databricks_pipeline.source_configurations: Array of objects describing top-level source configurations. See the [REST API docs](https://docs.databricks.com/api/workspace/pipelines/create#ingestion_definition-source_configurations) for reference
databricks_pipeline.storage: A location on cloud storage where output data and metadata required for pipeline execution are stored. By default, tables are stored in a subdirectory of this location. *Change of this parameter forces recreation of the pipeline.* (Conflicts with `catalog`)
databricks_pipeline.table_configuration: Configuration settings to control the ingestion of tables. These settings are applied to all tables in the pipeline
databricks_pipeline.tags: A map of tags associated with the pipeline. These are forwarded to the cluster as cluster tags, and are therefore subject to the same limitations. A maximum of 25 tags can be added to the pipeline
databricks_pipeline.target: The name of a database (in either the Hive metastore or in a UC catalog) for persisting pipeline output data. Configuring the target setting allows you to view and query the pipeline output data from the Databricks UI
databricks_pipeline.url: URL of the Lakeflow Declarative Pipeline on the given workspace
databricks_pipeline.user_name: The email of an active workspace user. Non-admin users can only set this field to their own email
databricks_pipeline.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_quality_monitor.assets_dir: - The directory to store the monitoring assets (Eg. Dashboard and Metric Tables)
databricks_quality_monitor.baseline_table_name: Name of the baseline table from which drift metrics are computed from.Columns in the monitored table should also be present in the baseline table
databricks_quality_monitor.custom_metrics: Custom metrics to compute on the monitored table. These can be aggregate metrics, derived metrics (from already computed aggregate metrics), or drift metrics (comparing metrics across time windows)
databricks_quality_monitor.dashboard_id: The ID of the generated dashboard
databricks_quality_monitor.data_classification_config: The data classification config for the monitor
databricks_quality_monitor.drift_metrics_table_name: The full name of the drift metrics table. Format: __catalog_name__.__schema_name__.__table_name__
databricks_quality_monitor.granularities: List of granularities to use when aggregating data into time windows based on their timestamp
databricks_quality_monitor.id: ID of this monitor is the same as the full table name of the format `{catalog}.{schema_name}.{table_name}`
databricks_quality_monitor.inference_log: Configuration for the inference log monitor
databricks_quality_monitor.input_columns: Columns on the monitored table to apply the custom metrics to
databricks_quality_monitor.model_id_col: Column of the model id or version
databricks_quality_monitor.monitor_version: The version of the monitor config (e.g. 1,2,3). If negative, the monitor may be corrupted
databricks_quality_monitor.name: Name of the custom metric
databricks_quality_monitor.notifications: The notification settings for the monitor.  The following optional blocks are supported, each consisting of the single string array field with name `email_addresses` containing a list of emails to notify:
databricks_quality_monitor.on_failure: who to send notifications to on monitor failure
databricks_quality_monitor.on_new_classification_tag_detected: Who to send notifications to when new data classification tags are detected
databricks_quality_monitor.output_data_type: The output type of the custom metric
databricks_quality_monitor.output_schema_name: - Schema where output metric tables are created
databricks_quality_monitor.prediction_proba_col: Column of the model prediction probabilities
databricks_quality_monitor.problem_type: Problem type the model aims to solve. Either `PROBLEM_TYPE_CLASSIFICATION` or `PROBLEM_TYPE_REGRESSION`
databricks_quality_monitor.profile_metrics_table_name: The full name of the profile metrics table. Format: __catalog_name__.__schema_name__.__table_name__
databricks_quality_monitor.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_quality_monitor.quartz_cron_expression: string expression that determines when to run the monitor. See [Quartz documentation](https://www.quartz-scheduler.org/documentation/quartz-2.3.0/tutorials/crontrigger.html) for examples
databricks_quality_monitor.schedule: The schedule for automatically updating and refreshing metric tables.  This block consists of following fields:
databricks_quality_monitor.skip_builtin_dashboard: Whether to skip creating a default dashboard summarizing data quality metrics.  (Can't be updated after creation)
databricks_quality_monitor.slicing_exprs: List of column expressions to slice data with for targeted analysis. The data is grouped by each expression independently, resulting in a separate slice for each predicate and its complements. For high-cardinality columns, only the top 100 unique values by frequency will generate slices
databricks_quality_monitor.table_name: - The full name of the table to attach the monitor too. Its of the format {catalog}.{schema}.{tableName}
databricks_quality_monitor.timestamp_col: Column of the timestamp of predictions
databricks_quality_monitor.timezone_id: string with timezone id (e.g., `PST`) in which to evaluate the Quartz expression
databricks_quality_monitor.type: The type of the custom metric
databricks_quality_monitor.warehouse_id: Optional argument to specify the warehouse for dashboard creation. If not specified, the first running warehouse will be used.  (Can't be updated after creation)
databricks_quality_monitor.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_query.apply_auto_limit: Whether to apply a 1000 row limit to the query result
databricks_query.catalog: Name of the catalog where this query will be executed
databricks_query.create_time: The timestamp string indicating when the query was created
databricks_query.date_range_value: (Block) Manually specified date-time range value.  Consists of the following attributes: * `start` (Required, String) - begin of the date range. * `end` (Required, String) - end of the date range
databricks_query.description: General description that conveys additional information about this query such as usage notes
databricks_query.dynamic_date_range_value: (String) Dynamic date-time range value based on current date-time.  Possible values are `TODAY`, `YESTERDAY`, `THIS_WEEK`, `THIS_MONTH`, `THIS_YEAR`, `LAST_WEEK`, `LAST_MONTH`, `LAST_YEAR`, `LAST_HOUR`, `LAST_8_HOURS`, `LAST_24_HOURS`, `LAST_7_DAYS`, `LAST_14_DAYS`, `LAST_30_DAYS`, `LAST_60_DAYS`, `LAST_90_DAYS`, `LAST_12_MONTHS`
databricks_query.dynamic_date_value: (String) Dynamic date-time value based on current date-time.  Possible values are `NOW`, `YESTERDAY`
databricks_query.enum_options: (String) List of valid query parameter values, newline delimited
databricks_query.enum_value: (Block) Dropdown parameter value. Consists of following attributes:
databricks_query.id: unique ID of the created Query
databricks_query.last_modifier_user_name: Username of the user who last saved changes to this query
databricks_query.lifecycle_state: The workspace state of the query. Used for tracking trashed status. (Possible values are `ACTIVE` or `TRASHED`)
databricks_query.multi_values_options: If specified, allows multiple values to be selected for this parameter. Consists of following attributes:
databricks_query.name: Literal parameter marker that appears between double curly braces in the query text
databricks_query.numeric_value: (Block) Numeric parameter value. Consists of following attributes:
databricks_query.parameter: Query parameter definition.  Consists of following attributes (one of `*_value` is required):
databricks_query.parent_path: The path to a workspace folder containing the query. The default is the user's home folder.  If changed, the query will be recreated
databricks_query.precision: Date-time precision to format the value into when the query is run.  Possible values are `DAY_PRECISION`, `MINUTE_PRECISION`, `SECOND_PRECISION`.  Defaults to `DAY_PRECISION` (`YYYY-MM-DD`)
databricks_query.prefix: Character that prefixes each selected parameter value
databricks_query.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_query.query_backed_value: (Block) Query-based dropdown parameter value. Consists of following attributes:
databricks_query.query_id: ID of the query that provides the parameter values
databricks_query.run_as_mode: Sets the "Run as" role for the object.  Should be one of `OWNER`, `VIEWER`
databricks_query.schema: Name of the schema where this query will be executed
databricks_query.separator: Character that separates each selected parameter value. Defaults to a comma
databricks_query.start_day_of_week: Specify what day that starts the week
databricks_query.suffix: Character that suffixes each selected parameter value
databricks_query.tags: Tags that will be added to the query
databricks_query.text_value: (Block) Text parameter value. Consists of following attributes:
databricks_query.title: Text displayed in the user-facing parameter widget in the UI
databricks_query.update_time: The timestamp string indicating when the query was updated
databricks_query.values: (Array of strings) List of selected query parameter values
databricks_query.warehouse_id: ID of a SQL warehouse which will be used to execute this query
databricks_query.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_recipient.activation_url: Full activation URL to retrieve the access token. It will be empty if the token is already retrieved
databricks_recipient.allowed_ip_addresses: Allowed IP Addresses in CIDR notation. Limit of 100
databricks_recipient.authentication_type: The delta sharing authentication type. Valid values are `TOKEN` and `DATABRICKS`
databricks_recipient.cloud: Cloud vendor of the recipient's Unity Catalog Metstore. This field is only present when the authentication_type is `DATABRICKS`
databricks_recipient.created_at: Time at which this recipient was created, in epoch milliseconds
databricks_recipient.expiration_time: Expiration timestamp of the token in epoch milliseconds
databricks_recipient.id: Unique ID of the recipient token
databricks_recipient.metastore_id: Unique identifier of recipient's Unity Catalog metastore. This field is only present when the authentication_type is `DATABRICKS`
databricks_recipient.name: Name of recipient. Change forces creation of a new resource
databricks_recipient.owner: Username/groupname/sp application_id of the recipient owner
databricks_recipient.properties_kvpairs: Recipient properties - object consisting of following fields: * `properties` (Required) a map of string key-value pairs with recipient's properties.  Properties with name starting with `databricks.` are reserved
databricks_recipient.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_recipient.region: Cloud region of the recipient's Unity Catalog Metstore. This field is only present when the authentication_type is `DATABRICKS`
databricks_recipient.sharing_code: The one-time sharing code provided by the data recipient
databricks_recipient.tokens: List of Recipient Tokens. This field is only present when the authentication_type is TOKEN. Each list element is an object with following attributes:
databricks_recipient.updated_at: Time at which this recipient was updated, in epoch milliseconds
databricks_recipient.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_registered_model.catalog_name: The name of the catalog where the schema and the registered model reside. *Change of this parameter forces recreation of the resource.*
databricks_registered_model.comment: The comment attached to the registered model
databricks_registered_model.id: Equal to the full name of the model (`catalog_name.schema_name.name`) and used to identify the model uniquely across the metastore
databricks_registered_model.name: The name of the registered model.  *Change of this parameter forces recreation of the resource.*
databricks_registered_model.owner: Name of the registered model owner
databricks_registered_model.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_registered_model.schema_name: The name of the schema where the registered model resides. *Change of this parameter forces recreation of the resource.*
databricks_registered_model.storage_location: The storage location under which model version data files are stored.  If the URL contains special characters, such as space, `&`, etc., they should be percent-encoded (space -> `%20`, etc.). *Change of this parameter forces recreation of the resource.*
databricks_registered_model.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_repo.branch: name of the branch for initial checkout. If not specified, the default branch of the repository will be used.  Conflicts with `tag`.  If `branch` is removed, and `tag` isn't specified, then the repository will stay at the previously checked out state
databricks_repo.commit_hash: Hash of the HEAD commit at time of the last executed operation. It won't change if you manually perform pull operation via UI or API
databricks_repo.git_provider: case insensitive name of the Git provider.  Following values are supported right now (could be a subject for a change, consult [Repos API documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/dev-tools/api/latest/repos.html)): `gitHub`, `gitHubEnterprise`, `bitbucketCloud`, `bitbucketServer`, `azureDevOpsServices`, `gitLab`, `gitLabEnterpriseEdition`, `awsCodeCommit`
databricks_repo.path: path to put the checked out Git folder. If not specified, , then the Git folder will be created in the default location.  If the value changes, Git folder is re-created
databricks_repo.patterns: array of paths (directories) that will be used for sparse checkout.  List of patterns could be updated in-place
databricks_repo.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_repo.tag: name of the tag for initial checkout.  Conflicts with `branch`
databricks_repo.url: The URL of the Git Repository to clone from. If the value changes, Git folder is re-created
databricks_repo.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with
databricks_repo.workspace_path: path on Workspace File System (WSFS) in form of `/Workspace` + `path`

databricks_schema.browse_only: Indicates whether the principal is limited to retrieving metadata for the associated object through the BROWSE privilege when include_browse is enabled in the request.
databricks_schema.catalog_type: The type of the parent catalog.
databricks_schema.created_at: Time at which this schema was created, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_schema.enable_predictive_optimization: Whether predictive optimization should be enabled for this object and objects under it.
databricks_schema.full_name: Full name of schema, in form of __catalog_name__.__schema_name__.
databricks_schema.name: Name of schema, relative to parent catalog.
databricks_schema.owner: Username of current owner of schema.
databricks_schema.properties: A map of key-value properties attached to the securable.
databricks_schema.schema_id: The unique identifier of the schema.
databricks_schema.storage_location: Storage location for managed tables within schema.
databricks_schema.storage_root: Storage root URL for managed tables within schema.
databricks_schema.updated_at: Time at which this schema was created, in epoch milliseconds.
databricks_schema.updated_by: Username of user who last modified schema.

databricks_secret.config_reference: (String) value to use as a secret reference in [Spark configuration and environment variables](https://docs.databricks.com/security/secrets/secrets.html#use-a-secret-in-a-spark-configuration-property-or-environment-variable): `{{secrets/scope/key}}`
databricks_secret.id: Canonical unique identifier for the secret
databricks_secret.key: (String) key within secret scope. Must consist of alphanumeric characters, dashes, underscores, and periods, and may not exceed 128 characters
databricks_secret.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_secret.scope: (String) name of databricks secret scope. Must consist of alphanumeric characters, dashes, underscores, and periods, and may not exceed 128 characters
databricks_secret.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_secret_acl.principal: principal's identifier. It can be: * `user_name` attribute of [databricks_user](user.md). * `display_name` attribute of [databricks_group](group.md).  Use `users` to allow access for all workspace users. * `application_id` attribute of [databricks_service_principal](service_principal.md)
databricks_secret_acl.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_secret_acl.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_secret_scope.id: The id for the secret scope object
databricks_secret_scope.initial_manage_principal: The principal with the only possible value `users` that is initially granted `MANAGE` permission to the created scope.  If it's omitted, then the [databricks_secret_acl](secret_acl.md) with `MANAGE` permission applied to the scope is assigned to the API request issuer's user identity (see [documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/dev-tools/api/latest/secrets.html#create-secret-scope)). This part of the state cannot be imported
databricks_secret_scope.name: Scope name requested by the user. Must be unique within a workspace. Must consist of alphanumeric characters, dashes, underscores, and periods, and may not exceed 128 characters
databricks_secret_scope.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_secret_scope.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_service_principal.active: Either service principal is active or not. True by default, but can be set to false in case of service principal deactivation with preserving service principal assets
databricks_service_principal.allow_cluster_create: Allow the service principal to have [cluster](cluster.md) create privileges. Defaults to false. More fine grained permissions could be assigned with [databricks_permissions](permissions.md#Cluster-usage) and `cluster_id` argument. Everyone without `allow_cluster_create` argument set, but with [permission to use](permissions.md#Cluster-Policy-usage) Cluster Policy would be able to create clusters, but within the boundaries of that specific policy
databricks_service_principal.allow_instance_pool_create: Allow the service principal to have [instance pool](instance_pool.md) create privileges. Defaults to false. More fine grained permissions could be assigned with [databricks_permissions](permissions.md#Instance-Pool-usage) and [instance_pool_id](permissions.md#instance_pool_id) argument
databricks_service_principal.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_service_principal.databricks_sql_access: This is a field to allow the service principal to have access to [Databricks SQL](https://databricks.com/product/databricks-sql) feature through [databricks_sql_endpoint](sql_endpoint.md)
databricks_service_principal.disable_as_user_deletion: Deactivate the service principal when deleting the resource, rather than deleting the service principal entirely. Defaults to `true` when the provider is configured at the account-level and `false` when configured at the workspace-level. This flag is exclusive to force_delete_repos and force_delete_home_dir flags
databricks_service_principal.display_name: This is an alias for the service principal and can be the full name of the service principal
databricks_service_principal.external_id: ID of the service principal in an external identity provider
databricks_service_principal.force: Ignore `cannot create service principal: Service principal with application ID X already exists` errors and implicitly import the specified service principal into Terraform state, enforcing entitlements defined in the instance of resource. _This functionality is experimental_ and is designed to simplify corner cases, like Azure Active Directory synchronisation
databricks_service_principal.force_delete_home_dir: This flag determines whether the service principal's home directory is deleted when the user is deleted. It will have no impact when in the accounts SCIM API. False by default
databricks_service_principal.force_delete_repos: This flag determines whether the service principal's repo directory is deleted when the user is deleted. It will have no impact when in the accounts SCIM API. False by default
databricks_service_principal.home: Home folder of the service principal, e.g. `/Users/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`
databricks_service_principal.id: Canonical unique identifier for the service principal (SCIM ID)
databricks_service_principal.repos: Personal Repos location of the service principal, e.g. `/Repos/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`
databricks_service_principal.workspace_access: This is a field to allow the service principal to have access to a Databricks Workspace
databricks_service_principal.workspace_consume: This is a field to allow the service principal to have access to a Databricks Workspace as consumer, with limited access to workspace UI.  Couldn't be used with `workspace_access` or `databricks_sql_access`

databricks_service_principal_role.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_service_principal_role.role: This is the role name, role id, or [instance profile](instance_profile.md) resource
databricks_service_principal_role.service_principal_id: This is the id of the [service principal](service_principal.md) resource

databricks_share.cdf_enabled: Whether to enable Change Data Feed (cdf) on the shared object. When this field is set, field `history_data_sharing_status` can not be set
databricks_share.content: The content of the notebook file when the data object type is NOTEBOOK_FILE. This should be base64 encoded. Required for adding a NOTEBOOK_FILE, optional for updating, ignored for other types
databricks_share.created_at: Time when the share was created
databricks_share.created_by: The principal that created the share
databricks_share.data_object_type: Type of the data object. Supported types: `TABLE`, `FOREIGN_TABLE`, `SCHEMA`, `VIEW`, `MATERIALIZED_VIEW`, `STREAMING_TABLE`, `MODEL`, `NOTEBOOK_FILE`, `FUNCTION`, `FEATURE_SPEC`, and `VOLUME`
databricks_share.history_data_sharing_status: Whether to enable history sharing, one of: `ENABLED`, `DISABLED`. When a table has history sharing enabled, recipients can query table data by version, starting from the current table version. If not specified, clients can only query starting from the version of the object at the time it was added to the share. *NOTE*: The start_version should be less than or equal the current version of the object. When this field is set, field `cdf_enabled` can not be set
databricks_share.id: the ID of the share, the same as `name`
databricks_share.name: The name of the partition column
databricks_share.op: The operator to apply for the value, one of: `EQUAL`, `LIKE`
databricks_share.owner: User name/group name/sp application_id of the share owner
databricks_share.partition: Array of partitions for the shared data
databricks_share.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_share.recipient_property_key: The key of a Delta Sharing recipient's property. For example `databricks-account-id`. When this field is set, field `value` can not be set
databricks_share.shared_as: A user-provided alias name for **table-like data objects** within the share. Use this field for: `TABLE`, `VIEW`, `MATERIALIZED_VIEW`, `STREAMING_TABLE`, `FOREIGN_TABLE`. **Do not use this field for volumes, models, notebooks, or functions** (use `string_shared_as` instead). If not provided, the object's original name will be used. Must be a 2-part name `<schema>.<table>` containing only alphanumeric characters and underscores. The `shared_as` name must be unique within a share. Change forces creation of a new resource
databricks_share.start_version: The start version associated with the object for cdf. This allows data providers to control the lowest object version that is accessible by clients
databricks_share.status: Status of the object, one of: `ACTIVE`, `PERMISSION_DENIED`
databricks_share.string_shared_as: A user-provided alias name for **non-table data objects** within the share. Use this field for: `VOLUME`, `MODEL`, `NOTEBOOK_FILE`, `FUNCTION`. **Do not use this field for tables, views, or streaming tables** (use `shared_as` instead). Format varies by type: For volumes, models, and functions use `<schema>.<name>` (2-part name); for notebooks use the file name. Names must contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores. The `string_shared_as` name must be unique for objects of the same type within a share. Change forces creation of a new resource
databricks_share.value: The value of the partition column. When this value is not set, it means null value. When this field is set, field `recipient_property_key` can not be set
databricks_share.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_sql_endpoint.auto_stop_mins: Time in minutes until an idle SQL warehouse terminates all clusters and stops. This field is optional. The default is 120, set to 0 to disable the auto stop
databricks_sql_endpoint.cluster_size: The size of the clusters allocated to the endpoint: "2X-Small", "X-Small", "Small", "Medium", "Large", "X-Large", "2X-Large", "3X-Large", "4X-Large", "5X-Large"
databricks_sql_endpoint.creator_name: The username of the user who created the endpoint
databricks_sql_endpoint.data_source_id: (Deprecated, will be removed) ID of the data source for this endpoint. This is used to bind an Databricks SQL query to an endpoint
databricks_sql_endpoint.enable_photon: Whether to enable [Photon](https://databricks.com/product/delta-engine). This field is optional and is enabled by default
databricks_sql_endpoint.enable_serverless_compute: Whether this SQL warehouse is a serverless endpoint. See below for details about the default values. To avoid ambiguity, especially for organizations with many workspaces, Databricks recommends that you always set this field explicitly
databricks_sql_endpoint.id: the unique ID of the SQL warehouse
databricks_sql_endpoint.max_num_clusters: Maximum number of clusters available when a SQL warehouse is running. This field is required. If multi-cluster load balancing is not enabled, this is default to `1`
databricks_sql_endpoint.min_num_clusters: Minimum number of clusters available when a SQL warehouse is running. The default is `1`
databricks_sql_endpoint.name: Name of the Databricks SQL release channel. Possible values are: `CHANNEL_NAME_PREVIEW` and `CHANNEL_NAME_CURRENT`. Default is `CHANNEL_NAME_CURRENT`
databricks_sql_endpoint.no_wait: Whether to skip waiting for the SQL warehouse to start after creation. Default is `false`. When set to `true`, Terraform will create the warehouse but won't wait for it to be in a running state before completing
databricks_sql_endpoint.num_active_sessions: The current number of clusters used by the endpoint
databricks_sql_endpoint.num_clusters: The current number of clusters used by the endpoint
databricks_sql_endpoint.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_sql_endpoint.spot_instance_policy: The spot policy to use for allocating instances to clusters: `COST_OPTIMIZED` or `RELIABILITY_OPTIMIZED`. This field is optional. Default is `COST_OPTIMIZED`
databricks_sql_endpoint.state: The current state of the endpoint
databricks_sql_endpoint.tags: Databricks tags all endpoint resources with these tags
databricks_sql_endpoint.warehouse_type: SQL warehouse type. See for [AWS](https://docs.databricks.com/sql/admin/sql-endpoints.html#switch-the-sql-warehouse-type-pro-classic-or-serverless) or [Azure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/sql/admin/create-sql-warehouse#--upgrade-a-pro-or-classic-sql-warehouse-to-a-serverless-sql-warehouse). Set to `PRO` or `CLASSIC`. If the field `enable_serverless_compute` has the value `true` either explicitly or through the default logic (see that field above for details), the default is `PRO`, which is required for serverless SQL warehouses. Otherwise, the default is `CLASSIC`
databricks_sql_endpoint.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_sql_table.catalog_name: Name of parent catalog. Change forces the creation of a new resource
databricks_sql_table.cluster_id: All table CRUD operations must be executed on a running cluster or SQL warehouse. If a cluster_id is specified, it will be used to execute SQL commands to manage this table. If empty, a cluster will be created automatically with the name `terraform-sql-table`. Conflicts with `warehouse_id`
databricks_sql_table.cluster_keys: a subset of columns to liquid cluster the table by. For automatic clustering, set `cluster_keys` to `["AUTO"]`. To turn off clustering, set it to `["NONE"]`. Conflicts with `partitions`
databricks_sql_table.data_source_format: External tables are supported in multiple data source formats. The string constants identifying these formats are `DELTA`, `CSV`, `JSON`, `AVRO`, `PARQUET`, `ORC`, and `TEXT`. Change forces the creation of a new resource. Not supported for `MANAGED` tables or `VIEW`
databricks_sql_table.id: ID of this table in the form of `<catalog_name>.<schema_name>.<name>`
databricks_sql_table.identity: Whether the field is an identity column. Can be `default`, `always`, or unset. It is unset by default
databricks_sql_table.name: User-visible name of column
databricks_sql_table.options: Map of user defined table options. Change forces creation of a new resource
databricks_sql_table.owner: User name/group name/sp application_id of the table owner
databricks_sql_table.partitions: a subset of columns to partition the table by. Change forces the creation of a new resource. Conflicts with `cluster_keys`
databricks_sql_table.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_sql_table.schema_name: Name of parent Schema relative to parent Catalog. Change forces the creation of a new resource
databricks_sql_table.storage_credential_name: For EXTERNAL Tables only: the name of storage credential to use. Change forces the creation of a new resource
databricks_sql_table.storage_location: URL of storage location for Table data (required for EXTERNAL Tables).  If the URL contains special characters, such as space, `&`, etc., they should be percent-encoded (space -> `%20`, etc.).  Not supported for `VIEW` or `MANAGED` table_type
databricks_sql_table.table_id: The unique identifier of the table
databricks_sql_table.table_type: Distinguishes a view vs. managed/external Table. `MANAGED`, `EXTERNAL` or `VIEW`. Change forces the creation of a new resource
databricks_sql_table.type: Column type spec (with metadata) as SQL text. Not supported for `VIEW` table_type
databricks_sql_table.view_definition: SQL text defining the view (for `table_type == "VIEW"`). Not supported for `MANAGED` or `EXTERNAL` table_type
databricks_sql_table.warehouse_id: All table CRUD operations must be executed on a running cluster or SQL warehouse. If a `warehouse_id` is specified, that SQL warehouse will be used to execute SQL commands to manage this table. Conflicts with `cluster_id`
databricks_sql_table.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_storage_credential.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_storage_credential.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_token.comment: (String) Comment that will appear on the user's settings page for this token
databricks_token.id: Canonical unique identifier for the token
databricks_token.lifetime_seconds: (Integer) The lifetime of the token, in seconds. If no lifetime is specified, then expire time will be set to maximum allowed by the workspace configuration or platform
databricks_token.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_token.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_user.active: Either user is active or not. True by default, but can be set to false in case of user deactivation with preserving user assets
databricks_user.allow_cluster_create: Allow the user to have [cluster](cluster.md) create privileges. Defaults to false. More fine grained permissions could be assigned with [databricks_permissions](permissions.md#Cluster-usage) and `cluster_id` argument. Everyone without `allow_cluster_create` argument set, but with [permission to use](permissions.md#Cluster-Policy-usage) Cluster Policy would be able to create clusters, but within boundaries of that specific policy
databricks_user.allow_instance_pool_create: Allow the user to have [instance pool](instance_pool.md) create privileges. Defaults to false. More fine grained permissions could be assigned with [databricks_permissions](permissions.md#Instance-Pool-usage) and [instance_pool_id](permissions.md#instance_pool_id) argument
databricks_user.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_user.databricks_sql_access: This is a field to allow the user to have access to [Databricks SQL](https://databricks.com/product/databricks-sql)  UI, [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one) and through [databricks_sql_endpoint](sql_endpoint.md)
databricks_user.disable_as_user_deletion: Deactivate the user when deleting the resource, rather than deleting the user entirely. Defaults to `true` when the provider is configured at the account-level and `false` when configured at the workspace-level. This flag is exclusive to force_delete_repos and force_delete_home_dir flags
databricks_user.display_name: This is an alias for the username that can be the full name of the user
databricks_user.external_id: ID of the user in an external identity provider
databricks_user.force: Ignore `cannot create user: User with username X already exists` errors and implicitly import the specific user into Terraform state, enforcing entitlements defined in the instance of resource. _This functionality is experimental_ and is designed to simplify corner cases, like Azure Active Directory synchronisation
databricks_user.force_delete_home_dir: This flag determines whether the user's home directory is deleted when the user is deleted. It will have not impact when in the accounts SCIM API. False by default
databricks_user.force_delete_repos: This flag determines whether the user's repo directory is deleted when the user is deleted. It will have no impact when in the accounts SCIM API. False by default
databricks_user.home: Home folder of the user, e.g. `/Users/mr.foo@example.com`
databricks_user.id: Canonical unique identifier for the user (SCIM ID)
databricks_user.repos: Personal Repos location of the user, e.g. `/Repos/mr.foo@example.com`
databricks_user.user_name: This is the username of the given user and will be their form of access and identity.  Provided username will be converted to lower case if it contains upper case characters
databricks_user.workspace_access: This is a field to allow the user to have access to a Databricks Workspace UI and [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one)
databricks_user.workspace_consume: This is a field to allow the user to have access only to [Databricks One](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/workspace/databricks-one#who-can-access-databricks-one).  Couldn't be used with `workspace_access` or `databricks_sql_access`

databricks_user_role.api: Specifies whether to use account-level or workspace-level API. Valid values are `account` and `workspace`. When not set, the API level is inferred from the provider host
databricks_user_role.role: Either a role name or the ARN/ID of the [instance profile](instance_profile.md) resource
databricks_user_role.user_id: This is the id of the [user](user.md) resource

databricks_vector_search_endpoint.budget_policy_id: The Budget Policy ID set for this resource
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.creator: Creator of the endpoint
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.endpoint_id: Unique internal identifier of the endpoint (UUID)
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.endpoint_status: Object describing the current status of the endpoint consisting of the following fields:
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.id: The same as the name of the endpoint
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.last_updated_timestamp: Timestamp of the last update to the endpoint (milliseconds)
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.last_updated_user: User who last updated the endpoint
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.name: Name of the Mosaic AI Vector Search Endpoint to create. (Change leads to recreation of the resource). * `endpoint_type` (Required) Type of Mosaic AI Vector Search Endpoint.  Currently only accepting single value: `STANDARD` (See [documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/api/workspace/vectorsearchendpoints/createendpoint) for the list of currently supported values). (Change leads to recreation of the resource)
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.num_indexes: Number of indexes on the endpoint
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.state: Current state of the endpoint. Currently following values are supported: `PROVISIONING`, `ONLINE`, and `OFFLINE`
databricks_vector_search_endpoint.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_vector_search_index.columns_to_sync: (optional) list of columns to sync. If not specified, all columns are syncronized
databricks_vector_search_index.creator: Creator of the endpoint. * `delta_sync_index_spec`:
databricks_vector_search_index.delta_sync_index_spec: (object) Specification for Delta Sync Index. Required if `index_type` is `DELTA_SYNC`. This field is a block and is [documented below](#delta_sync_index_spec-Configuration-Block)
databricks_vector_search_index.direct_access_index_spec: (object) Specification for Direct Vector Access Index. Required if `index_type` is `DIRECT_ACCESS`. This field is a block and is [documented below](#direct_access_index_spec-Configuration-Block)
databricks_vector_search_index.embedding_model_endpoint_name: The name of the embedding model endpoint
databricks_vector_search_index.embedding_source_columns: (required if `embedding_vector_columns` isn't provided) array of objects representing columns that contain the embedding source.  Each entry consists of:
databricks_vector_search_index.embedding_vector_columns: (required if `embedding_source_columns` isn't provided)  array of objects representing columns that contain the embedding vectors. Each entry consists of:
databricks_vector_search_index.embedding_writeback_table: (optional) Automatically sync the vector index contents and computed embeddings to the specified Delta table. The only supported table name is the index name with the suffix `_writeback_table`
databricks_vector_search_index.endpoint_name: (required) The name of the Mosaic AI Vector Search Endpoint that will be used for indexing the data
databricks_vector_search_index.id: The same as the name of the index
databricks_vector_search_index.index_type: (required) Mosaic AI Vector Search index type. Currently supported values are: * `DELTA_SYNC`: An index that automatically syncs with a source Delta Table, automatically and incrementally updating the index as the underlying data in the Delta Table changes. * `DIRECT_ACCESS`: An index that supports the direct read and write of vectors and metadata through our REST and SDK APIs. With this model, the user manages index updates
databricks_vector_search_index.index_url: Index API Url to be used to perform operations on the index
databricks_vector_search_index.message: Message associated with the index status
databricks_vector_search_index.model_endpoint_name_for_query: The name of the embedding model endpoint which, if specified, is used for querying (not ingestion)
databricks_vector_search_index.name: The name of the column
databricks_vector_search_index.pipeline_id: ID of the associated Delta Live Table pipeline
databricks_vector_search_index.pipeline_type: Pipeline execution mode. Possible values are: * `TRIGGERED`: If the pipeline uses the triggered execution mode, the system stops processing after successfully refreshing the source table in the pipeline once, ensuring the table is updated based on the data available when the update started. * `CONTINUOUS`: If the pipeline uses continuous execution, the pipeline processes new data as it arrives in the source table to keep the vector index fresh
databricks_vector_search_index.primary_key: (required) The column name that will be used as a primary key
databricks_vector_search_index.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_vector_search_index.ready: Whether the index is ready for search
databricks_vector_search_index.schema_json: The schema of the index in JSON format.  Check the [API documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/api/workspace/vectorsearchindexes/createindex#direct_access_index_spec-schema_json) for a list of supported data types
databricks_vector_search_index.status: Object describing the current status of the index consisting of the following fields:
databricks_vector_search_index.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_volume.access_point: The AWS access point to use when accesing s3 for this external location.
databricks_volume.browse_only: Indicates whether the principal is limited to retrieving metadata for the associated object through the BROWSE privilege when include_browse is enabled in the request.
databricks_volume.catalog_name: The name of the catalog where the schema and the volume are
databricks_volume.comment: The comment attached to the volume
databricks_volume.created_by: The identifier of the user who created the volume
databricks_volume.full_name: The three-level (fully qualified) name of the volume
databricks_volume.metastore_id: The unique identifier of the metastore
databricks_volume.name: The name of the volume
databricks_volume.owner: The identifier of the user who owns the volume
databricks_volume.schema_name: The name of the schema where the volume is
databricks_volume.storage_location: The storage location on the cloud
databricks_volume.updated_by: The identifier of the user who updated the volume last time
databricks_volume.volume_id: The unique identifier of the volume
databricks_volume.volume_type: The type of the volume. An external volume is located in the specified external location. A managed volume is located in the default location which is specified by the parent schema, or the parent catalog, or the Metastore. [Learn more]

databricks_workspace_binding.binding_type: Binding mode. Default to `BINDING_TYPE_READ_WRITE`. Possible values are `BINDING_TYPE_READ_ONLY`, `BINDING_TYPE_READ_WRITE`
databricks_workspace_binding.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_workspace_binding.securable_name: Name of securable. Change forces creation of a new resource
databricks_workspace_binding.securable_type: Type of securable. Can be `catalog`, `external_location`, `storage_credential` or `credential`. Default to `catalog`. Change forces creation of a new resource
databricks_workspace_binding.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with

databricks_workspace_file.content_base64: The base64-encoded file content. Conflicts with `source`. Use of `content_base64` is discouraged, as it's increasing memory footprint of Terraform state and should only be used in exceptional circumstances, like creating a workspace file with configuration properties for a data pipeline
databricks_workspace_file.object_id: Unique identifier for a workspace file
databricks_workspace_file.path: The absolute path of the workspace file, beginning with "/", e.g. "/Demo"
databricks_workspace_file.provider_config: Configure the provider for management through account provider. This block consists of the following fields:
databricks_workspace_file.source: Path to file on local filesystem. Conflicts with `content_base64`
databricks_workspace_file.url: Routable URL of the workspace file
databricks_workspace_file.workspace_id: Workspace ID which the resource belongs to. This workspace must be part of the account which the provider is configured with
databricks_workspace_file.workspace_path: path on Workspace File System (WSFS) in form of `/Workspace` + `path`
